Get seal of passage from Brundt the Chieftain, sail to Lunar Isle with Captain Bentley (talk to 5 crew NPCs in strict order, then find 5 hidden symbols with a lit lantern around the ship). On Lunar Isle: kill Suqah (safespottable, lvl 111) for a tooth, brew a waking-sleep potion for Baba Yaga/Oneiromancer. Charge a Dramen staff at all 4 elemental altars (air/fire/water/earth, in order) to make a Lunar staff, hand to Oneiromancer. Mine+smelt lunar ore into a lunar helm; solve Pauline Polaris name-guess dialogue; kill more Suqah for a special tiara + 4 hides, tan hides, craft full lunar gear (torso/trousers/gloves/boots); dig up blue flowers west of the two bridges for the lunar ring; give the Oneiromancer all lunar gear then get it back. Enter the Dream World: light ceremonial brazier with waking-sleep-soaked kindling, then complete 6 mini-puzzles at colored platforms (dice-sum guessing, number-sequence pattern, 20-log chopping race, memorize-and-cross a Dream Puffs stepping-stone maze, hurdle race, copy-the-emote) before fighting and killing Me (level 79, safespottable, no gear switching allowed once in the dream, can't teleport out). Return via lectern to Lunar Isle, hand in seal of passage to Oneiromancer to finish.

The 100-cast god-spell unlock afterward is the real time cost, not the boss fight itself. Deep Wilderness (level 53-56) throughout -- PKer risk, and 'PK Skull Prevention' setting plus hidden attack option are recommended before entering.

Spawn locations rotate every ~45 minutes of Wilderness time, per-player. High PKer risk in deep Wilderness; dying to a player loses the cape (killer gets 60,000gp).

Chapter II: infiltrate Glough's dungeon via the Agility Route or the Tanking Route (web-slashing) to reach and kill Kruk (level 207). Chapter III: defeat Kob (185) and Keef (178), sneak into Ape Atoll disguised as Assistant Le Smith, and play the Monkey Gear Solid stealth minigame back to Kruk's dungeon. Chapter IV: major combat/story chapter (6933B walkthrough). Chapter V: 3-phase final battle strategy against Glough (431) with combat-style-dependent tanking or safespotting per phase. Very high spine value -- unlocks the heavy ballista and Crash Site Cavern demonic gorillas, both major mid-late-game combat gates.

name: Monk's Friend

items: regular logs or a regular plank

unlocks: 8 law runes
Brother Omad throws repeatable parties when spoken to

notes: Short novice fetch quest: run past thieves (two level-14, one level-26) in a stone-circle den west of the monastery to recover a stolen child's blanket, then help Brother Cedric prep a birthday party by giving him a jug of water and some logs/a plank before returning to Brother Omad.

passage into Mountain Camp over the rockslide
The Kendal accessible in Nightmare Zone
required for The Fremennik Exiles

notes: Find the chieftain's missing daughter for the isolated Mountain Camp tribe: pole-vault and mud-climb a wilderness route to a talking magic pool, broker peace between the camp and Rellekka (mine a sample of ancient rock, get safe passage), find a hardy food plant on White Wolf Mountain, then kill The Kendal (level 70, melee-only, safespottable) in a cave to recover the daughter's remains and give her a proper burial.

name: Mourning's End Part I

HP to 20 on contact -- ring of dueling to Ferox pool of refreshment recommended afterward). Flow: talk Eluned then Arianwyn in Lletya, kill a Mourner at the Arandar gate and loot its 7-piece outfit. Clean the bloody mourner top with Tegid's stolen soap + bucket of water at Taverley. Mend trousers with silk+bear fur via Oronwen in Lletya (buy dyes here). Don full mourner gear, enter West Ardougne Mourner HQ, free/befriend the caged gnome downstairs with feather+leather+magic log+toad crunchies. Re-dye 4 sheep herds (Feldip Hills or Castle Wars swamp toads dyed via ogre bellows) using the fixed device's Aim-and-Fire mode on the coloured sheep north of East Ardougne. Make a barrel of toxic powder (naphtha + rotten apple mush via sieve, cooked on a range not fire) and poison 2 of West Ardougne's food stores, then report to Essyllt and Arianwyn to finish. Sets up Mourning's End Part II (teleport crystal) and unlocks the Death Altar chain via that sequel.

name: Mourning's End Part II

items: Death talisman/tiara, catalytic talisman/tiara, Runecraft cape, OR 50 junk items

unlocks: Crystal trinket (re-enter Temple of Light after the quest)
Access to the Death Altar
Death talismans obtainable via Guardians of the Rift
Death runes craftable during Guardians of the Rift
Access to Dark Beasts
Dark Beasts teleport added to Slayer ring menu

notes: No hard skill requirement, but 65+ combat, 75-91 Agility (higher = fewer obstacle failures), and 43+ Prayer are strongly recommended for the shadow-infested temple. Flow: in mourner gear, get the new key from Essyllt, traverse a cave to reach the Temple of Light, chip a piece off a black crystal and have Arianwyn enchant it. Re-enter the temple and solve a 3-floor light/mirror puzzle across 5 numbered treasure chests plus a final Death Altar puzzle -- each chest step repositions hand mirrors and coloured crystals (yellow/cyan/blue/fractured) to route a light beam to unlock the next chest, ultimately opening a path to the Death Altar. Ironmen without a death talisman must instead collect 50 junk items to open the Death Altar route. Charge the enchanted crystal at the Death Altar, then use it on the black crystal to finish and return to Arianwyn.

name: Murder Mystery

notes: No requirements (Kandarin-region quest, included in this bundle's spine). Old man Sinclair is dead; find flypaper and a dagger inside the mansion, use flour+flypaper on silver items from two thread-colour-matched suspects' bedrooms to dust prints and match one to the dagger. Ask Gossip outside and the Seers' Village pub poison salesman who bought poison, confront the matched suspect about their motive, then search the item they claim to have used the poison on to confirm before reporting to a guard.

name: Natural history quiz

notes: Talk to Orlando Smith to start, then click the plaque in front of each of 14 creature displays (North room: Lizard/Battle tortoise/Dragon/Wyvern; East room: Snail/Snake/Sea slug/Monkey; South room: Kalphite Queen/Terrorbird; West room: Penguin/Mole/Camel/Leech) and answer 3 multiple-choice questions correctly per display -- answers come from that display's own Natural historian's lecture or the wiki page text itself, and a good approach is simply guessing since only 3-of-several need to be right. Once all 3 for a display are answered correctly its questions are replaced by the creature's common+scientific name. Return to Orlando after all 14 displays are done for the xp reward.

name: Nature Spirit

notes: Intermediate quest, direct Priest in Peril sequel. Talk to Drezel for pies, run south through Mort Myre Swamp (must enter via the gate or Filliman won't appear) to the Nature Grotto north-west of Mort'ton, wear the ghostspeak amulet and talk to Filliman, find a mirror under a bench's washing bowl and use it on him, then search the grotto tree for his journal and hand it back for a druidic spell. Return to Drezel to be blessed, cast the spell next to a rotting log in the swamp, bring the mushroom you pick to Filliman's camp, solve his 3-stone puzzle (mushroom on the light-brown stone, used spell card on the grey stone, stand on the orange stone), enter the hole under the tree and give the nature spirit your silver sickle for a druid pouch. Use the sickle's Bloom ability on swamp plants to fill the pouch, use the filled pouch on ghasts to make them attackable and kill three of them, then return to the nature spirit to finish. Required for: Fairytale I - Growing Pains, In Search of the Myreque, Recipe for Disaster (Lumbridge Guide subquest), While Guthix Sleeps, Easy Morytania Diary.

unlocks: A random constellation reward, assigned once per account and fixed thereafter: pool includes combat-skill xp (~875 in one combat stat, pure-account protected from assignment below level ~3 for most XP signs post-Nov 2018), runes, an amulet of defence, a black 2h sword, a maple longbow, weapon poison, or a super strength(1) -- exact assignment is per-account RNG, not player choice
Permanent Agility shortcut through the Observatory dungeon once Agility 23/Ranged 24/Strength 28 (boostable) + crossbow + mith grapple are available

An unlit antipoison spawns near the dungeon entrance if you didn't bring one.

access to brine rat cave
parchment leading into The Graveyard (upcoming quest)

Agreeing phase: Yanni Salika -> Jungle forester (blunt axe) -> Brian in Port Sarim (sharpen axe) -> Aggie in Draynor -> Johanhus Ulsbrecht in the H.A.M. Hideout -> Fred the Farmer -> Seth Groats (chicken farm) -> Horvik in Varrock (give 3 steel bars) -> the Apothecary -> Tassie Slipcast (Barbarian Village) -> Hammerspike Stoutbeard (Dwarven Mine) -> brew a Guthix rest tea for Sanfew (Taverley herblore shop) -> Captain Bleemadge (White Wolf Mountain gnome glider) -> Arhein (Catherby docks) -> Phantuwti Fanstuwi Farsight (Seers' Village) -> search a sculpture in the Goblin Cave SE of the Fishing Guild -> Wizard Cromperty (NE Ardougne) -> Tindel Marchant (Port Khazard) -> Rantz (Feldip Hills, mattress) -> Gnormadium Avlafrim (cut/replace gems in landing lights, crushed gems cost 500gp each to replace, up to 2). Completing phase: report back to Gnormadium and Rantz; give Tindel the mattress; give Cromperty iron oxide; use an animate rock scroll on the sculpture to fight and kill Slagilith, free Petra, and finish her dialogue; repair Phantuwti's weather vane (3 parts smithed on an anvil with bronze/iron/steel bars) and deliver the forecast to Arhein then Bleemadge then Sanfew; kill Hammerspike's 3 gang members then make a pot lid (via Tassie's clay) for the Apothecary; deliver pigeon cages via Horvik, Seth Groats, Johanhus, Aggie, Brian, then the Jungle forester; finally return to Yanni Salika with the sharpened-axe red mahogany to finish.

Accept a shipwreck-salvage job from Will/Anne, helm a ship south through the Bay of Sarim to east of the Karamja Shipyard (trim sails on wind gusts for speed), deploy the salvaging hook. At the Pandemonium Pub talk to 'Squawking' Steve Beanie and Ribs about the map, then get a raft from Junior Jim in exchange for a salvaged cup; build a cargo hold on the raft with the hammer+saw. Final leg: sail the raft north through Mudskipper Sound/Bay of Sarim to Port Sarim, load cargo from the ledger table (must have empty hands, no weapons), sail back and deliver it. Talk to Steve Beanie to finish.

notes: The dwarven town Cam Torum's underground temple Neypotzli has stirred 3 sealed ancient bosses. Kill a Sulphur Nagua guarding the entrance, enter the city, and meet Jessamine, Attala, Zuma. Build 3 camps in the dungeon's corner areas (Ancient Prison, Streambound Cavern, Earthbound Cavern) using building supplies. Get water and earth talismans from Attala, have them infused (magic shop's Nahta, then the blacksmith), then dowse hot/cold with both talismans to locate the hidden NPC Eyatlalli (location varies per player). Gather 3 ritual items around the dungeon (moonlight grub paste, bream scales from fishing, a trapped moss lizard's tail) and start the ritual. Fight all 3 Moons of Peril in their own chambers: Blue Moon (crush-weak; weapon-freeze and brazier-relight specials), Blood Moon (slash-weak; raining-blood pools and blood-jaguar specials), Eclipse Moon (stab-weak; rotating shield and clone-facing specials) -- stand in a rotating safe-damage circle throughout, damage during specials still counts. Return to the Antechamber and speak to Eyatlalli to finish.

Return to Port Sarim, get a white apron from Gerrant's Fishy Business, work for Wydin to retrieve the rum from the crate, and trade it to Redbeard Frank for a key. Use the key on a chest upstairs in Varrock's Blue Moon Inn to read a pirate message pointing to Falador Park. Dig there (kill/evade the Gardener) to unearth the treasure casket.

His wife Alrena makes a gas mask from dwellberries; soften the mud behind the house with 4 buckets of water then dig under the wall with the spade, use rope on the blocked pipe grill to get through. Inside, show Jethick Elena's picture, learn from the Rehnison family she was taken to the barred plague house. Cure Bravek's hangover (mix chocolate dust + bucket of milk + snape grass) to get a warrant past the Mourners guarding that house, find and free Elena, then return to Edmond via the manhole. One of the Ardougne-diary prereq quests alongside Sheep Shearer and Clock Tower.

Required for: Animal Magnetism, Creature of Fenkenstrain, Desert Treasure I, Ghosts Ahoy, Haunted Mine, His Faithful Servants, Making History, Nature Spirit, Rum Deal, Shades of Mort'ton, Wanted!.

Talk your way past the level-26 jail guards with 3 beers for Joe, use the rope on Lady Keli, unlock the cell with the smelted key, and hand the wig/paste/pink skirt to Prince Ali so he can disguise himself out. Report back to Hassan to finish.

Buying 27 extra vinegar jugs now (35 total) saves a trip if planning Rag and Bone Man II next; direct prerequisite for that sequel quest.

Kill 27 varied monsters worldwide (bats, undead cows, werewolves, ghouls, zombies, moss giants, jackals, desert lizards, vultures, ice giants, mogres, snakes, jogres, baby blue dragons, trolls, rabbits, basilisks (needs mirror shield), dagannoths, fire giants, terrorbirds, wolves, ogres, zogres, etc.) for their bones, then repeat the vinegar-soak-and-boil process from part I 27 times at the Odd Old Man's hut. name: Recruitment Drive

items: None required, but you MUST have an empty inventory and nothing equipped to be allowed into the testing grounds

unlocks: Initiate armour access (free sallet, then buy cuisse 8,000gp / hauberk 10,000gp / full set 20,000gp from Sir Tiffy Cashien)
Gaze of Saradomin: choice to respawn at Falador instead of Lumbridge on death, toggleable via Sir Tiffy Cashien
Initiate title in the Order of Temple Knights -- gates Wanted!

notes: First quest in the Temple Knight series. After Sir Amik sends you to Sir Tiffy Cashien in Falador Park, you must clear 5 of 7 possible randomized puzzle rooms in a row (order and selection random per playthrough) -- always including a Sir Leye fight (use the warhammer or fight unarmed; any bladed weapon on the killing blow fails the room). Other rooms are logic/memory puzzles (river-crossing animals, missing-statue memory test, multi-step chemistry door puzzle, riddle-based combination lock, multiple-choice riddles, an 8-second stand-still test). Failing any room sends you back to Falador to restart the 5-room gauntlet from scratch. Short quest but gates Wanted! and the Medium Falador Diary, so it's a real progression choke point despite its low difficulty rating.

name: Regicide

items: Strip of cloth (4 wool on a loom)
Cooked rabbit (obtained in-quest) or roast rabbit

unlocks: 15,000 coins
Access to the elven kingdom of Tirannwn and the Arandar overpass
Wield the dragon halberd (60 Attack/30 Strength)
Charter a ship to, or dock a boat at, Port Tyras (66 Sailing)
Iorwerth camp teleport scrolls
Zul-Andra teleport scrolls
BJS fairy ring code
Attempt the Gwenith Glide (Barracuda Trial)

notes: Agility 56 is only needed to avoid a slower alternate route through the traps (not a hard start gate); must also defeat a safespottable level 110 Tyras guard. Flow: return through the Underground Pass (west West-Ardougne entrance) to Iban's Temple exit, run west through Isafdar meeting Idris then Lord Iorwerth at the Iorwerth Camp, track the Elf Tracker to find/kill the Tyras guard for a crystal pendant, collect coal-tar barrels + sulphur, gather quicklime/naphtha ingredients and distill naphtha at the Rimmington chemist's still (careful tar/pressure/coal-feed minigame), assemble a barrel bomb (quicklime+sulphur+naphtha+cloth), carry it back through the Underground Pass to the catapult north of Tyras Camp, bait the guard with cooked rabbit and fire the bomb to destroy Lord Iorwerth's target, then return to King Lathas to finish. Directly prerequisite for Roving Elves and the rest of the Mourner/elf storyline.

name: Rogue Trader

quests: Icthlarin's Little Helper (only needs to be STARTED, for Sophanem access)
Gertrude's Cat (prerequisite of Icthlarin's Little Helper)
items: 3 dyes of the same colour OR 3 animal pelts of the same kind (cowhide, grey wolf fur, snakeskin, bear fur, or wool -- not balls of wool) to unlock either Menaphite clothing or the Desert outfit
100 coins, optional, to bribe the street urchin for defensive blackjacks

unlocks: Ali Morrisane's rune stock (opened via casket runedoku puzzle / Strength force / Thieving pick on a small casket, puzzle-only on the large casket)
Offensive or defensive blackjack stock (switchable later)
Menaphite clothing or Desert outfit stock (switchable later, cosmetic-only variants of Desert clothing)
Discounted magic carpet network fares: 100gp (75gp with ring of charos (a)), down from 200gp/100gp

notes: Not an official miniquest -- the page itself says it does not appear in the quest list; wiki classifies it as one-off 'training or trade unlock' content, not a Quest List miniquest. Three independent errand branches, doable in any order, each separately unlocking a piece of Ali's shop: (1) Runes -- solve/force/pick two lockable rune caskets (small casket allows any method, large casket ONLY the runedoku puzzle); (2) Blackjacks -- talk to the Blackjack seller in Pollnivneach, pick offensive (take 2 hits) or defensive (bribe an urchin) style; (3) Clothes -- give Siamun in Sophanem 3 dyes or 3 matching pelts. All three done = full access to Ali's Discount Wares.

name: Romeo & Juliet

unlocks: 5 quest points only -- no xp/item rewards listed on the page

notes: No requirements. Classic go-between quest: relay a letter from Juliet (upper balcony of the mansion west of Varrock's west bank) to Romeo in the square, then fetch cadava berries (grow west of the SE Varrock mine) for the Apothecary near the west bank to brew a sleeping potion. Give the potion to Juliet and tell Romeo to meet her in the crypt to finish. Prerequisite quest feeding into Defender of Varrock's requirement chain.

name: Roving Elves

items: Glarial's pebble (obtained in-quest if not kept from Waterfall Quest)

unlocks: Crystal shield OR crystal bow with 500 charges (player choice)
Moss Guardian becomes accessible in the Nightmare Zone
Isafdar painting for the Quest hall (required for the hard Western Provinces Diary)

notes: Agility 56 is waived if travelling to the elven woods by charter ship instead of on foot. Must also be able to defeat a level 84 Moss Guardian unarmed under heavy item restrictions (flinchable). Flow: track down and befriend Islwyn (world-hop if not present), bank combat gear, use Glarial's pebble on Glarial's tombstone NW of the Fishing Guild, kill the Moss Guardian for a consecration seed, then travel through the Waterfall Dungeon (raft+rope+rope+door) to plant the seed in the final room and report back to Islwyn for the reward choice. Directly sets up Mourning's End Part I's teleport crystal if Eluned is then spoken to.

name: Royal Trouble

items: 5 coal (obtainable during quest with a pickaxe, also obtainable)

unlocks: 20,000 coins
Bosun Zarah hireable as crewmate at Sailing 80
Enhanced rewards from Managing Miscellania
ability to dock at Etceteria at Sailing 65

notes: Second Miscellania-series quest. As regent, investigate thefts threatening war between Miscellania and Etceteria; talking to citizens on both sides reveals soldiers demanded tithes and were refused. King Vargas sends you to the Sailor at the docks, who mentions visiting children; get a signed scroll from Vargas to access the dungeon under Miscellania Castle. Talk to the dwarf Donal, then repair a broken lift using an engine + 5 coal, 3 beams, 3 pulley beams and 2 ropes assembled in a specific order. Deeper in, swing across water with a rope, cross slippery rocks with a plank (avoid steam vents and falling rocks), and collect 5 pages of a burnt diary from fire remains, reading it before the final crevice. Meet the runaway Fremennik-trial children (a Scooby-Doo parody) who admit to staging the whole conflict, then kill the Giant Sea Snake (level 149, 100 HP, poison hits up to 9 -- antipoison strongly recommended) in an instanced boss room. Collect the heavy box it drops, then deliver a letter from Queen Sigrid to King Vargas to finish. reqs.quests is the fully flattened chain from the page's own Details template (Throne of Miscellania requires Heroes' Quest which requires Shield of Arrav+Lost City+Merlin's Crystal+Dragon Slayer I, plus The Fremennik Trials).

name: Rum Deal

items: seed dibber (obtainable in-quest, or partial Barbarian Training farming section)
at least 47 prayer points for the evil spirit fight

unlocks: Holy wrench (grants extra prayer points from prayer potions while held)
access to Braindeath Island

notes: Must be able to kill a level 150 monster (safespottable). Zogre Flesh Eaters' own Big Chompy Bird Hunting/Jungle Potion->Druidic Ritual chain is inherited/flattened. Talk to Pirate Pete then Captain Braindeath (decline his money) to start; grab a blindweed seed. Plant blindweed in the south-eastern patch, wait for it to grow, feed it into the brewery hopper. Fill a bucket with stagnant water north of the island and add it to the hopper. Fish 5 sluglings near the farm patch and add them, then pull the lever. Get Davey to bless your wrench (needs 47+ Prayer) and use it on the shaking brewing control, then kill the level 150 Evil spirit (safespottable). Kill a fever spider (level 49, safespottable) and add its corpse to the hopper. Collect the finished 'Unsanitary Swill' and deliver it to Captain Donnie among the protesters, then report back to Captain Braindeath to finish.

name: Rune Mysteries

unlocks: ability to mine rune essence
an air talisman
lamps/books of knowledge from random events can go into Runecraft
5 Kudos (claim from Historian Minas, Varrock Museum)

notes: No requirements or items needed -- easiest of the orphan gates. Duke Horacio gives an air talisman; take it to Archmage Sedridor in the Wizards' Tower basement, who sends it on to Aubury in his Varrock rune shop as a research package; Aubury swaps it for notes to carry back to Sedridor, who then explains Runecraft basics and returns the talisman. The single most-required prereq quest on the wiki: opens rune essence mining/Runecraft entirely, and directly gates Aubury's essence-mine teleport (Varrock Diary Easy task) and Sedridor's essence-mine teleport (Lumbridge & Draynor Diary Easy task).

name: Sacred Bone Burner

items: Five unnoted dragonstones, OR 100 (baby)dragon bones for a half-restore / 200 for a full restore (lava dragon bones count double, superior dragon bones count triple toward the 200 total)

rewards.xp: ?? (burner grants +300% Prayer xp on eligible bone/ash offerings once faith reaches 100+ points, permanently)
unlocks: The Sacred Bone Burner's boosted-xp offering effect
a temple key that permanently unlocks the remaining gates/shortcut in Forthos Dungeon

notes: One-off restoration errand, not on the quest list. Olbertus's burner/shrine sits under a curse; offer eligible draconic bones (babydragon, dragon, lava dragon, superior dragon) to raise its 'faith level' from 0 toward 200 points (giving 5 unnoted dragonstones up front instead jumps you straight to the 100-point mark). Faith thresholds give flavour messages at 0/50/100/150/200; at 100 the burner starts granting +300% Prayer xp for offered bones/ashes; at 200 a temple key appears, permanently unlocking the dungeon's remaining gates/shortcut, though the xp boost itself has no further threshold beyond 100. Use the 'Check-Faith' option on the burner to gauge progress at any time.

name: Scorpion Catcher

items: Dusty key (obtainable during quest) or Agility 70 (boostable) to bypass shortcut

unlocks: Thormac can enchant a battlestaff into a mystic staff for 40,000 coins

notes: Thormac's three rare Kharid Scorpions have escaped; he gives a scorpion cage. Talk to a Seer in Seers' Village to unlock the hidden room in Taverley Dungeon holding scorpion 1 -- reach it past blue dragons (anti-dragon shield recommended) and black demons/poison spiders, using a dusty key from the jailer (or the 70/80 Agility shortcuts to skip). Scorpion 2 is upstairs in Edgeville Monastery (needs Prayer 31 to enter, one-time). Scorpion 3 is in the Barbarian Outpost Agility Course (needs Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl completed). Catch each with the cage, then return the full cage to Thormac.

name: Scrambled

unlocks: Egg pet (from talking to the egg on the wall, can be placed in pet house)
Alan's bones lootable south of temple (auto-buried for 1.5 prayer xp if wearing bonecrusher/bonecrusher necklace)

notes: Alan asks you to mind his egg-friend Humpy while he cooks a feast; gravity kills it the moment he leaves. Recruit three of King's men from The King's Inn in Tal Teklan to help rebuild the egg: Acatzin (repair blacksmith's whetstone then his axe), Nezketi (brew damiana tea: pick damiana leaves + bowl of water from fountain, boil on oven, pour into cup), Kauayotl (repair his cart with 2 planks, nails from blacksmith's workbench, hammer, saw from Archery Supplies). Back at the temple the three can't fix the original egg so you fetch three replacement eggs by killing and looting egg piles: large chicken (lvl16, chicken farm south of temple), red dragon (lvl106, Dragon Nest SE across the river, safespottable), black jaguar (lvl88, campsite east, safespot behind tropical tree/fern). None of the painted eggs survive judging (three more great falls), so you and King's men solve a sliding/rotate puzzle (hover puzzle-piece icon for the solution) to reassemble the original egg. Cutscene resolves the plot; ironically Alan himself then has a great fall. Avoid having a bonecrusher equipped if you want his bones as a keepsake instead of auto-crushed prayer xp.

name: Sea Slug

skills: Firemaking 30 (boostable, not required at quest start -- only needed later to light the torch)
items: Swamp paste (made by cooking flour+swamp tar on a fire; also buyable from a Trader crewmember 75gp, Rasolo 60gp west of Fishing Guild, or Khazard General Store 42gp at full stock)

unlocks: Access to the Fishing Platform
Oyster pearls

notes: First quest in the Sea Slug series and #3 in the Temple Knight series. Give Holgart swamp paste to fix his boat and reach the Fishing Platform; find Bailey to learn sea slugs fear heat, rescue Kennith's father Kent from a nearby island, then light a torch (needs Firemaking 30, boostable) using broken glass + damp sticks, and use it to save Kennith from the platform machinery. CORRECTION to the depth:04 queue payload: its rationale text calls this quest's unlock 'Fishing Guild access' -- the quest's own Rewards section states the unlock is 'Access to the Fishing Platform', not the Fishing Guild; these are different locations and the brief's phrasing appears to be an error. Gates The Slug Menace (sequel).

name: Secrets of the North

items: Lockpick (the Strange Old Lockpick does NOT work)

unlocks: Mine ancient essence crystals in the Ghorrock Dungeon
Access to a stronger version of the Strange Creature: the Phantom Muspah

notes: Also needs Desert Treasure I started (for the ring of visibility) though not a full completion requirement. Murder-mystery continuing the Hazeel Cult/General's Shadow storyline. Investigate Ceril's murder at Carnillean Mansion (broken window, hidden room behind a false wall), then track and fight Evelot (level 148, drains prayer on her specials) near the Fight Arena bar by following a clue trail. Visit the Hazeel Cult hideout via an Ardougne sewers raft to enlist Hazeel's help, lockpick a hidden chest behind the mansion's cooking shelves for a scroll. Travel to Weiss, question Big Fish and Snowflake, then fight a level-262 Assassin (lures you with smoke bombs, throws poison vials). Solve a multi-stage code-lock dungeon under Weiss (a 'BLOOD' gate code, a chest code, a brazier-lighting order, an arrow-direction gate code, combining two jewel shards) to reach the final boss, the Strange Creature -- resupply with ranged/magic gear first. Return to the mansion guard to finish; unlocks the Phantom Muspah boss.

name: Shades of Mort'ton

items: 2 Tarromin potion (unf) OR 2 vials of water + 2 grimy tarromin (both obtainable in-quest)
Logs or an axe to cut some
Hammer (a Flamtaer hammer is highly recommended — buyable from Razmire for ~13,000 coins — since a regular hammer alone can't finish the temple repair if the minigame world is empty)

unlocks: Ability to play the Shades of Mort'ton minigame
Shades of Mort'ton minigame teleport
Extra 335 Herblore xp for turning in the Diary of Herbi Flax to the Apothecary in Varrock (once only)

notes: Search the shelf in the ruined SW house of Mort'ton and read the diary found there to start (this also unlocks making Serum 207). Make 2 doses of Serum 207 (clean tarromin + vial of water, then add ashes), use one on Razmire (afflicted) in the general store, kill 5 loar shades for their remains, return the remains to Razmire. Buy an olive oil from his general store and 5 timber beams/5 limestone bricks/25 swamp paste (+ optional Flamtaer hammer) from his builders' store. Serum the sick Ulsquire Shauncy east of the store and talk to him twice about the remains/temple. Head to the temple NE of Mort'ton and repair it to 100% with at least 20 sanctity (minigame, safer with other players present), light the sacred altar (or wait for someone with 5+ Firemaking), pour olive oil on the flame for sacred oil, and dip Serum 207 in the flame for permanent-cure Serum 208. Make a pyre log with the sacred oil, take it plus loar remains to one of the stone funeral pyre groups west of the general store, light the pyre (25 Prayer xp), collect the reward, then cure both Razmire and Ulsquire with Serum 208 to finish.

name: Shadow of the Storm

items: 3+ pieces of black outfit (black desert robe/shirt can be dyed in-quest; dyed Silverlight does not count; void gear does not count)
black dye (grind a black mushroom with pestle+mortar and a vial)
means to enter the desert (Shantay pass, desert amulet 2, teleports, fairy rings, or Pharaoh's sceptre)

unlocks: 10,000xp in any combat skill other than Prayer (player's choice)
Silverlight upgrades into Darklight (special attack, stronger vs demons, costlier to replace)
up to 6 cut gems (2 sapphire/2 ruby/2 emerald) from the demon's throne if not already taken during The Golem
Agrith-Naar accessible in Nightmare Zone

notes: Must be able to defeat a level 100 demon (Agrith-Naar). Talk to Father Reen, travel to the Ruins of Uzer (magic carpet, necklace of passage, or fairy ring DLQ), talk to Father Badden (with Silverlight in inventory), dye Silverlight black with a black mushroom/dye, grab a strange implement, then talk to Evil Dave wearing 3 black items plus the dyed Silverlight to join the cult. Through the portal: talk to Denath, get a demonic sigil mould from Jennifer, ask Matthew about Josef, smelt a demonic sigil from a silver bar at a furnace, swap out the black outfit for combat gear (keep Silverlight), ask the clay golem about the prior night, find and read a book hidden in one of 4 kilns and deliver it to Matthew, get the incantation from Denath (write it down, used twice), complete the ritual circle and chant it, retrieve the demonic sigil Denath drops, exit through the portal and take Tanya's sigil, then tell Evil Dave to return to the lair (4 sigils total). Rally the troops: convince Father Badden and Father Reen, reprogram the golem with the strange implement and send it into the dungeon. Final fight: say the incantation backwards to Matthew, kill Agrith-Naar (level 100, flinchable behind the NW torch) with any weapon but land the final blow with Silverlight (don't flee mid-final-blow or it heals), then unequip Silverlight and choose a reward skill to finish.

name: Shadows of Custodia

items: 4 willow longbows (buy from Arcuani's Archery Supplies in Tal Teklan, or Lowe's in Varrock)

unlocks: Access to the Stalker Den (Custodian stalkers)

notes: Missing-persons mystery in Auburnvale. Read the pub noticeboard, talk to Marcus, the Bartender, Ictus, and Captain Ariadna, then to Francis/Aemilia about their missing children. Follow red marks west to the river, fish cloth from a floating plank, then follow a trail of puddles around the mountain to a cave holding an injured boy. Bring 4 willow longbows to Captain Ariadna and reinforce the house's west wall with 4 maple logs + a hammer, then talk to the now-rescued kids upstairs. Return to the cave, talk to Antos, and fight 3 Strange Creatures (level 93 -- their bleed still ticks through Protect from Melee). Report back to Captain Ariadna to finish and unlock the Stalker Den.

name: Sheep Shearer

items: 20 balls of wool (unnoted) -- shears if shearing/spinning your own

xp.Crafting: 150 (200 if the balls of wool are spun on a spinning wheel instead of bought)

unlocks: 60 coins

notes: No requirements. Fred wants 20 balls of wool. Fastest route is simply buying 20 balls of wool (tradeable) and handing them in; the 'intended' route -- shear sheep in the pen with shears, spin the wool at a spinning wheel -- is optional and grants +50 bonus Crafting xp over just buying. Same opener tier as Cook's Assistant, designed to walk new players through Lumbridge.

name: Shield of Arrav

unlocks: 600 coins
Ability to claim 5 Kudos + an Antique lamp (1,000 xp in any skill level 20+) from Historian Minas after completion (members only)

notes: Talk to Reldo in the Varrock Palace library about quests, check the bright blue book on the top shelf of the bookcase, read it, then talk to Reldo again to start. You must then pick a side, Phoenix Gang or Black Arm Gang, and need a partner going the opposite gang to finish (official chat-channel 'OSRS SOA' helps pair players). Gang-specific inner steps ?? (walkthrough branches into two separate subpages, not fetched at quick-guide grain). Once your half of the shield is retrieved, take it to Curator Haig Halen in Varrock Museum, trade certificate halves with your partner (ironman: use halves on partner), combine the two halves, then hand the completed certificate to King Roald for the reward.

name: Shilo Village

items: lit torch/candle (item is lost, lanterns/firemaking cape don't work)
ability to survive undead ones (level 61-73) and defeat Nazastarool's 3 forms (~level 90, all safespottable)

unlocks: Shilo Village access
cart travel to Shilo Village from Brimhaven docks
sell quest items to Yanni Salika in Shilo Village
charter the Lady of the Waves to Port Khazard/Port Sarim
Nazastarool accessible in the Nightmare Zone
required for Recipe for Disaster - Freeing Sir Amik Varze
required for Legends' Quest
required for Lunar Diplomacy
required for One Small Favour
Medium and Hard Karamja Achievement Diary tasks

notes: Use a wampum belt on Trufitus in Tai Bwo Wannai to properly start the quest, then explore the temple of Ah Za Rhoon for a stone-plaque/scrolls/corpse, the Tomb of Bervirius on Cairn Isle for a locating crystal and bone beads, and finally the Tomb of Rashiliyia where you fight the undead queen Nazastarool across 3 forms; return her corpse to Trufitus's dolmen to finish.

name: Sins of the Father

items: Vyrewatch outfit top/legs/shoes (or 1,950 coins to buy it from Trader Sven mid-quest)
Runes for Lvl-3 Enchant (1 cosmic + 5 fire) or an enchant ruby/topaz tablet

unlocks: Access to Darkmeyer (Daeyalt Essence Mine, Hallowed Sepulchre, Vyre-noble shopping, buildable long-rope Agility shortcuts)
Tome of experience: 15,000 xp to any skill level 60+, reusable 6 times (recoverable from Ivan Strom if lost)
Blisterwood flail
Darkmeyer teleport via Drakan's medallion
Boat travel between Burgh de Rott, Slepe, and the Icyene Graveyard
Access to the mutated-bloodveld section of Meiyerditch Laboratories
True Blood Altar access with a blood talisman, including the Guardian of Blood in Guardians of the Rift
Promotion to Lieutenant of the Myreque

notes: Continues the Myreque/vampyre storyline after Ranis Drakan's death. Investigate Slepe's sleeper plague (tail Carl by hiding in sequence to find his supplier, kill lab-assistant Kroy from behind cover with ranged, destroy both lab tables). Trek with Ivan Strom through the swamp (vine bridge, kill nail beasts, repair a log bridge, kill low-tier vampyres with the Ivandis flail) to Burgh de Rott. Boat to the Icyene Graveyard, solve a mausoleum-door puzzle, brief the Myreque council, then mine 15 Daeyalt ore in the Ver Sinhaza mines and help Safalaan retrieve a book past a mutated bloodveld. Disguise as a Vyre noble (buy/upgrade the outfit via Polmafi Ferdygris) to enter Meiyerditch, then fight Damien Leucurte (stomp out his periodic magic fires or your hits do nothing) with the Ivandis flail. Cut 8 blisterwood logs in Darkmeyer's Arboretum after solving a two-valve gallon puzzle, forge the Blisterwood flail (enchanted ruby sickle + blisterwood log + Ivandis flail), then fight the endgame boss Vanstrom Klause in two phases: phase 1 dodge his acid-bloodveld/blood-orb-heal/stare-into-darkness specials under Protect from Magic; phase 2 (after he self-heals 200hp and drops specials) dodge repeated lightning strikes since prayer no longer blocks his damage. Unlocks Darkmeyer city.

name: Skippy and the Mogres

items: Nettle tea (bowl of water + nettles, cooked -- gloves needed to pick nettles, extra nettles recommended in case of burning at low Cooking)
Hangover cure or its ingredients: chocolate dust (chocolate bar + knife/pestle and mortar), bucket of milk, snape grass

unlocks: Ability to fight/be assigned Mogres as Slayer at level 32 (required for Falador Medium diary + Rag and Bone Man II)

notes: Cure a drunk fisherman cursed by mogres. Sober him up by throwing a bucket of water on him, use nettle tea on him (careful not to drink it yourself), talk to him, then brew a hangover cure by combining chocolate dust into a bucket of milk and adding snape grass (again don't drink the intermediate 'chocolatey milk'), and use the finished cure on Skippy to finish. No combat required for the miniquest itself; the unlock (mogres as a valid Slayer task/kill) is used afterward, recommended with 32 Slayer + fishing explosives.

name: Song of the Elves

items: Flowers (from mithril/adamant seeds) or an irit leaf
Wine of zamorak or Zamorak brew(4)

unlocks: Access to Prifddinas (Zalcano, The Gauntlet, Trahaearn Mine, Iorwerth Dungeon, respawn-point option, move POH there, teleport crystal, new spirit tree destination, divine potions, crystal tools/armour/weapons)
Prifddinas port + Port Tyras noticeboard access

refs.title: Song of the Elves/Warped Library quick guide

notes: Grandmaster capstone that unlocks Prifddinas. Disguise as an Ardougne knight (dyed helm + tabard) to free Elena from Ardougne Castle's dungeon, infiltrate West Ardougne as a mourner to stir unrest (burn grain sacks, talk to townsfolk), then switch back to knight gear to rile up East Ardougne merchants. Fight two waves of guards ('Battle of West Ardougne' at Mourner HQ, 'Battle of East Ardougne' at the castle), triggering exile to Lletya. Learn Arianwyn's rebellion plan, then solve a six-pillar light puzzle at Baxtorian Falls (6 items keyed to per-pillar riddle clues) to reach the Well of Voyage. Grow a cadantine seed, get a crystal seed enchanted, then track down Lord Amlodd and Lady Trahaearn (repairing an elderly elf's mechanism with a runite bar) to reach the Enchanted Valley and brew an inversion potion. Solve the Grand Library's nine house-seal puzzles (separate sub-guide), then find Lord Crwys and Lady Hefin, retrieve an orb of light by killing two Iorwerth guards, track Ysgawyn via a footprint/clue trail, and survive the attack on Lletya -- a bow fight against Arianwyn (dodge orange 1x1 bomb arrows, re-enable prayers after purple ones). Solve a five-symbol clue chain across Lletya and outdoor dig sites to find Lady Ithell and Lady Meilyr, then defend a dwarf camp for 4 minutes inside the Underground Pass and beat Essyllt, before the finale: a magic-only boss fight against the Fragment of Seren with 4 recurring specials (teleport-to-boss, copy/split identification, spawned healing orbs to snipe, and a near-max-hit freeze nuke requiring overheal via Saradomin brews). Completing it opens Prifddinas.

name: Spirit of Adventure

items: Logs and a tinderbox (obtainable during the quest)

unlocks: None (lore-only per both the quest's own Rewards section and the Miniquests page's Lore table)

notes: Unnamed-in-game miniquest (config name spiritofadventure_*), released 23 July 2025 with Varlamore: The Final Dawn. Wearing a ghostspeak amulet, talk to ghost Bernina, who died in the wreck that named Shipwreck Cove and lost her diamond necklace to a salvager. Find salvager Jasper at Salvager Overlook (he sold it on due to bad luck) to learn it went to a mountain climber. Grab logs+tinderbox from houses south of Jasper, climb the Proudspire to find climber Bernard, light a fire for him (with the logs/tinderbox) to learn he's carrying the necklace up the mountain to honour his late mother -- who turns out to be Bernina. Return to Bernina to relay this; she teleports to join Bernard atop the Proudspire. Return to Bernard so you can ghost-interpret for the reunion, then climb to the peak obelisk and speak to Bernard there to finish. No combat, no item/xp reward.

name: Spirits of the Elid

items: Crush weapon, stab weapon, slash weapon (or a spear/hasta/bladed staff for all 3)
Ranged or Magic attack method (37 Ranged needed even for magic option)

unlocks: Nardah fountain water access
Nardah Shrine to Elidinis - prayer restores HP/boosts it temporarily/cures poison+venom/restores prayer+run energy+special attack energy
Robe of elidinis

notes: Must beat 3 level-75 golems (white/grey/black), each weak to a different melee style (stab/slash/crush respectively) or a ranged/magic water-channel puzzle. Talk Awusah then Ghaslor the Elder for the ballad, find torn robe top+bottom in a cupboard, repair with needle+thread and equip (Robes of Elidinis), telegrab an ancestral key. Follow the river north to a waterfall cave, rope the overhanging root, use the ancestral key wearing the robes to open the door. Kill white golem (stab) then clear its water channel via Thieving; kill grey golem (slash) then clear via pickaxe; kill black golem (crush) then clear via Ranged/Magic on a target. Talk to a spirit through the north door, return to Nardah. Separately: take a genie's shoes by Awusah's door, knife them for a sole, find a crevice west of the general store, rope it, talk to and strike a deal with the Genie for a statuette, then place the statuette on the shrine plinth north of the fountain to finish. Region-opener (Nardah/desert), no known deeper dependency; useful all-purpose restoration shrine unlock.

name: Swan Song

items: airtight pot (pot+pot lid, or soft clay/pickaxe to make one in-quest)
5 iron bars (or pickaxe to mine own)
logs of any kind (or axe to chop own)

unlocks: 25,000 coins
access to Piscatoris Fishing Colony
ability to fish monkfish
Skeleton Mages fightable for training
free use of Kathy Corkat's boat
on-request brown apron/bone seeds from Malignius Mortifer

notes: One Small Favour's chain (Rune Mysteries, Shilo Village -> Jungle Potion -> Druidic Ritual) and Garden of Tranquillity's chain (Creature of Fenkenstrain -> Priest in Peril, The Restless Ghost) are prerequisites, flattened. Must be able to defeat a combat level 170 monster (Sea Troll Queen: magic+melee, drains prayer). Talk to Herman Caranos, then the Wise Old Man in Draynor, then return to the colony (fight through level 79 sea trolls, collect 7 bones, enter and speak to Herman). Repair the colony: light the firebox, press iron bars into sheets to repair the perimeter wall, fish and cook 5 monkfish for Arnold Lydspor while fending off increasingly tough sea trolls, then talk to the Wise Old Man/Herman. Raise an army: visit Wizard Frumscone in the Wizards' Guild basement, then Malignius Mortifer south of Falador (get brown apron), make a pot+lid at the Crafting Guild into an airtight pot (don't wear Saradomin gear near Malignius), teleport back to the colony. Final battle: fight and kill the Sea Troll Queen (Protect from Magic while meleeing, or use ranged). Note: starting this quest temporarily blocks the Sir Amik Varze RFD subquest until 'Repairing the Colony' is done; the colony is instanced during the quest so Treasure Trail unlocks only apply after full completion.

name: Tai Bwo Wannai Trio

items: iron spear or better (consumed during quest)
ranged/magic/halberd to safely kill a monkey for its corpse
Karamjan rum or 30 coins to buy one

unlocks: 2,000 coins
karambwan-poisoned rune spear
access to Tamayu's Spear Stall
access to Tiadeche's Karambwan Stall
ability to pray at the tribal statue in Tai Bwo Wannai
ability to catch/cook karambwans
ability to fight the Shaikahan (level 83, needs a kp spear)
Tai Bwo Wannai teleport scrolls usable
Barbarian Training miniquest smithing section unlocked

notes: Jungle Potion's own Druidic Ritual requirement is inherited/flattened. Prep ahead: banana rum (slice a banana onto Karamjan rum bought from Zembo at Musa Point, quest-safe to teleport with), burnt jogre bones (burn jogre bones with a tinderbox, needs 30 Firemaking or use a furnace), pasty jogre bones (fish 45 raw karambwanji near fairy ring CKR, grind one into paste, apply to burnt bones, cook - don't bury), and a karambwan-poisoned spear (cook a raw karambwan into a poison karambwan, grind it, apply paste to spear). Talk to Timfraku to start, then to Lubufu (help him fish, learn Karambwan fishing, lose/regain a vessel for a spare). Head to fairy ring DKP: give Tiadeche a baited karambwan vessel, then go to CKR and find Tamayu at the mine - give him the agility potion and poisoned spear, watch him fight the Shaikahan, then bring him a monkey corpse and a seaweed-monkey-skin sandwich. Cross to Cairn Isle and give Tinsay the rum, sandwich, and marinated jogre bones for a crafting manual; give the manual to Tiadeche to get him to agree to return. Return to Timfraku to finish; optionally talk to Tiadeche, Tinsay, and Tamayu afterward for extra XP/rewards.

name: Tale of the Righteous

items: runes for 2-3 casts of any magic combat spell

unlocks: 8,000 coins
page for Kharedst's memoirs (teleport to Graveyard of Heroes, SW of Shayzien bank)
ability to recolour graceful outfit to Shayzien colours
Corrupt Lizardman fightable in Nightmare Zone
3 Xerician fabric

notes: Client of Kourend's own X Marks the Spot requirement is inherited/flattened. Combat level ~20 vs a level 46 Corrupt Lizardman (poison-capable) recommended. Talk to Phileas Rimor and agree to help, then travel to the Arceuus Library (Archeio) to be teleported into the Tower of Magic prison via Pagida; solve a 3-attack-type puzzle on a strange device (push west, attack with magic from north, attack with melee from south, push east, attack with ranged from south), then inspect a skeleton in the northern cell. Report to Phileas, then to Lord Shiro Shayzien at the War Tent. Travel to the summit of Mount Quidamortem, speak to Historian Duffy, rope down a western crevice, mine and push a boulder, kill the Corrupt Lizardman guarding a magic gate, inspect an Unstable Altar, world-hop to leave, then speak to Duffy and Gnosi together back at the altar. Report to Lord Shiro Shayzien, then return to Phileas's now-empty house for dialogue, then back to Lord Shiro Shayzien once more to finish.

name: Tears of Guthix

items: Lit sapphire lantern, or the parts to make one (bullseye lantern/bullseye lantern (unf), cut sapphire, tinderbox, lamp oil from the Rimmington chemist or Dorgesh-Kaan lamp still)
Rope (only if this is your first entry to the Lumbridge Swamp Caves and you lack The Lost Tribe progress)

unlocks: Ability to play the Tears of Guthix minigame once per week -- a repeatable activity that grants xp in the player's currently lowest skill each run, giving it ongoing long-run passive-xp value beyond the one-time quest reward

notes: 43 QP gate makes this a mid-game unlock, not an early one. Tell Juna stories to earn a stone bowl recipe, then use a light creature (attracted by the sapphire lantern) to cross the chasm to a rock vein, mine a magic stone, chisel it into a stone bowl, and hand it to Juna to finish. The sapphire lantern doubles as the light source needed to safely navigate the caves and is reusable later for While Guthix Sleeps. Gates While Guthix Sleeps and the Hard Lumbridge & Draynor Diary. Note: 'Tears of Guthix' the quest and 'Tears of Guthix' the repeatable minigame it unlocks are the same wiki title-root but two distinct pages -- cited both above since the depth:04 brief's long-run-value framing refers to the minigame, not the quest itself.

name: Temple of Ikov

items: yew bow or better (any bow that fires ice arrows) or a thrown weapon
means to reach -1kg weight (boots of lightness, obtainable in-quest, or graceful outfit)

unlocks: Armadyl pendant (good path, requires killing Guardians of Armadyl)
Pendant of Lucien (door access in Temple of Ikov)
optional Boots of lightness
optional shiny key (dungeon shortcut to McGrubor's Wood)
5 kudos (claim from Historian Minas, Varrock Museum)

notes: Must be able to defeat a level 84 Fire Warrior of Lesarkus with Ranged. Choice quest with a good path (side with Guardians of Armadyl, kill Lucien level 14) or evil path (steal the Staff of Armadyl for Lucien, may need to kill a safespottable level 43 Guardian of Armadyl). Enter the temple dungeon south of the Ranging Guild; get boots of lightness from behind a web (needs light source+knife), equip Pendant of Lucien to pass the north gate, cross a lava bridge at -1kg or less, take a lever, cross back and attach the lever to a bracket then pull it, loot ice arrows from chests along an icy path (16 arrows enough with Ava's + 60 Ranged). Go back through the north gate, search-and-pull another lever (needs 42 Thieving), fight through to the Fire Warrior of Lesarkus (level 84, kill with ice arrows, Protect from Magic negates damage), then hand Winelda 20 unnoted limpwurt roots at once to be teleported across the lava (one-way, no going back - caution for ironmen). Optionally grab a shiny key for a dungeon shortcut. Push through to the Guardians of Armadyl room and pick a side to finish.

name: Temple of the Eye

xp.Runecraft: 5,000 direct + ~4,210 gained during the quest itself, ~9,210 total

unlocks: Access to the Guardians of the Rift minigame
Medium pouch (if not already owned)

notes: Short intro quest gating Guardians of the Rift. Get the eye amulet from Wizard Persten, use it on the Mage of Zamorak at Varrock's Altar of Chaos, buy (don't steal, or a 10-minute lockout) a strong tea from the Tea Seller to bribe him into an Abyss teleport, then use the amulet on the Dark Mage and solve a colour-matching rift puzzle (touch runic colours until all are white; order is random per player) to receive an abyssal incantation. Return to Persten, teleport to the Wizards' Tower, hand the incantation to Archmage Sedridor, then solve three apprentice riddles with Traiborn (the answer is 11). Finish with a guided Guardians of the Rift tutorial: assemble/charge guardians, mine and grind guardian fragments into essence, and power the Great Guardian at the Mind and Water altars.

name: The Ascent of Arceuus

unlocks: 2,000 coins
Page for Kharedst's memoirs 'A dark disposition' (teleports to the centre of Arceuus)
Ability to recolour the graceful outfit to Arceuus colours
Fight the Trapped Soul in the Nightmare Zone

notes: The first death in Arceuus house in a thousand years needs investigating. Talk to Mori, report it to Councillor Andrews at Kourend Castle, then go to the Tower of Magic and fight through 5 Tormented Souls (level 16) to reach Asteros Arceuus upstairs. Travel to Mount Karuulm, talk to Kaal-Xil-Dar, then find an ancient grave SE of the mountain and follow a track (searching bushes/plants along the way) to a hidden Trapped Soul (level 30) and defeat it. Report back to Kaal-Xil-Dar, then search the rocks ringing the Dark Altar north of Arceuus to find and destroy a hidden device. Finish by talking to Lord Trobin Arceuus back at the Tower of Magic.

name: The Blood Moon Rises

items: Vyre noble clothing (free in-quest from Myreque Hideout chest)

rewards.xp: Tome of experience granting 30,000 xp to any skill 70+, usable 6 times
unlocks: Access to Vampyrium (Maggot King boss, bloodwood trees, seeking arrows, leechfin fishing, stymphike/letvek hunting, venator slaying)
Hallowed flail
Sunspear
New fermenting vat at the restored Burgh de Rott Myreque pub (run by Veliaf Hurtz)
Ivan Strom moves to Paterdomus selling vampyrebane weapons (Efaritay takes over his old role)
Vyres tolerant of the player before the final fight — Darkmeyer services usable without a vyre noble outfit, Vyrewatch mine escort without mining ore/wearing the outfit
Vyrewatch Sentinels no longer aggressive by default (reactivate via garlic cocktails bought from a werewolf south of the teleport)

notes: Grandmaster capstone finale to the vampyre/Myreque questline. Lord Lowerniel Drakan, the last 'Blood Moon', is trying to reclaim the vampyre throne and destroy Morytania to do it. Player works through Castle Drakan's key-and-clock puzzles, a running Blood Hunt chase through the woods, defends Another Night at the Theatre, then fights a multi-phase final battle culminating in Wyrd and then Drakan himself across three phases before the sun rises.

name: The Corsair Curse

unlocks: Access to Yusuf's bank in Corsair Cove
Ability to dock your player-owned boat at Corsair Cove Port
Corsair Traitor fightable in the Nightmare Zone

notes: Talk to Captain Tock at the crossroads north of Port Sarim, agree to help, then meet him again on the single dock west of Rimmington to sail to Corsair Cove. On arrival speak to Ithoi the Navigator first (west along the beach, upstairs) or the other NPC dialogues break. Talk to Arsen the Thief and Cabin Boy Colin (same upstairs room, first house west of docks) and Gnocci the Cook (upstairs NW building, grab a tinderbox and a spade nearby if needed) about their 'curses'. Get the ogre artefact from Captain Tock, return it to Chief Tess in the Corsair Cove Dungeon. Dig a sand lump near the tropical tree by the fishing spot, use the telescope on Ithoi's hut deck, then report findings back to Gnocci, Arsen, and Colin. Question the same four NPCs again about who's the real traitor, culminating in confronting Ithoi twice, torching driftwood under his hut with your tinderbox to catch him not-sick, and reporting to Captain Tock. Enter the instanced fight and kill Ithoi the Navigator (level 35, protect from magic negates his damage), then report the kill to Captain Tock to finish. Recommended 20+ Combat plus weapon/armour/food for the fight.

name: The Curse of Arrav

items: Mith grapple (hallowed grapple will not work)

unlocks: Access to Zemouregal's Fort

notes: Master-tier Mahjarrat-lore quest continuing the vampyre/Arrav storyline. Elias White helps you find a way to free Arrav from Zemouregal's necromantic control: raid the Uzer Mastaba tomb (lever/trap puzzle, golem guard, coloured-tile crossing) for heart-preservation knowledge, sneak through Trollweiss Mountain tunnels into Zemouregal's old fort and briefly meet a still-captive Arrav, then infiltrate Zemouregal's Base — insulated boots past energy surges, a 4-digit vault code puzzle, and a crossbow-grapple fire-trap crossing — to steal Arrav's heart, before fighting a mind-controlled Arrav while dodging venom pools left by armoured zombies.

name: The Depths of Despair

items: Ability to kill a level 36 Sand Snake

unlocks: 4,000 coins
Kharedst's memoirs page 'Lunch by the lancalliums' (teleport east of the Hosidius Kitchen)
Ability to recolour the graceful outfit to Hosidius colours
Ability to fight the Sand Snake in the Nightmare Zone

notes: Short intermediate quest: Lord Kandur's son Artur Hosidius has gone missing. Interview the household (Chef Olivia reveals a secret romance and a library research lead), find a book in the Arceuus Library pointing to the sunken Accord of Twill, brave the Crabclaw Caves' stepping-stone crossing to find Artur trapped by a level 36 Sand Snake (weak to magic), kill it, and return the recovered Royal Accord of Twill to Lord Kandur. Required for: A Kingdom Divided, Kourend & Kebos Medium Diary.

name: The Dig Site

items: Cup of tea (stealable from Varrock tea stall) or nettle tea
Opal or uncut opal (small chance while panning for a quest item)
Specimen brush, specimen jar, panning tray, trowel (all obtained in-quest)

unlocks: 2 gold bars

Choice of Fruit blast (needed for Recipe for Disaster: Another Cook's Quest) or chocolate cake (needed for Desert Treasure I) from the museum curator per certificate handed in — note the reward is lost if you exit dialogue after he takes the certificate but before choosing
Ability to do Varrock Museum specimen-cleaning minigame for a Digsite pendant
Ancient talisman viewable in the Digsite Exhibit at the museum ground floor

notes: Talk to an examiner to attempt (and auto-fail) a first exam, then get a sealed letter from Curator Haig Halen at Varrock Museum. North of the Exam Centre near the blue urn hilltop, search bushes for a teddy bear, take a panning tray, use a cup of tea on the panning guide, pan for a Special cup (and an opal if needed), and pickpocket the nearby workman for an animal skull and specimen brush. Deliver the teddy/cup/skull to the three named students around the dig (NW, E, S respectively, talk twice each). Retake all 3 exams at the Exam Centre picking the correct dialogue options (fail is not possible once you know the answers) to get Level 1/2/3 certificates, re-visiting the students between exams (the female student demands an opal for the 2nd). Save the certificates for the museum curator. Back at the Exam Centre's western room, get a rock pick and specimen jar from cupboards, use the trowel on soil in a level-3 dig site until you find an ancient talisman (can take a while), then talk to Terry Balando and use his invitation on any digsite workman except the one by the western winch. Rope down the western winch to grab arcenia root and search bricks, then rope down the northern winch to talk to Doug Deeping about the rocks. South of the site, unlock a chest for chemical powder, get an unidentified liquid from a barrel (careful — it deals 25 damage and breaks the vial if dropped), grind charcoal, then give the powder/liquid to Terry Balando and mix nitroglycerin+ammonium nitrate, then charcoal, then arcenia root into a chemical compound. Use the compound then a tinderbox on the bricks down the western winch, enter the chamber (watch for level-22 skeletons), grab the stone tablet, and hand it to Terry Balando to finish.

name: The Enchanted Key

items: Enchanted key (reward item from Making History; re-buyable for 500 coins from the silver merchant in East Ardougne if lost)

unlocks: Saradomin mjolnir, Guthix mjolnir, Zamorak mjolnir (only non-trade source of these unique weapons)

60 iron ore, 30 mithril ore, 263 pure essence, 20 mind/60 earth/50 fire/30 water/30 law/15 death runes, 20 iron/40 steel/30 mithril arrows, 40 iron arrowtips (repeatable-ish treasure across ~11 dig sites)

notes: Hot-and-cold treasure hunt across ~11 fixed sites Gielinor-wide (map pins include areas near Rellekka, Ardougne, Tree Gnome Stronghold, Falador, Varrock, Mudskipper Point, Lumbridge Swamp, Al Kharid). Use 'Feel Enchanted key' to get a two-part temperature reading: an absolute distance band (freezing/very cold/cold/warm/hot/very hot/burning hot/steaming, based on Chebyshev distance ignoring obstacles) plus a relative warmer/colder/same-as-last-reading hint; walk in a direction, compare readings, and triangulate N/S then E/W until 'the key is steaming' (within 4 squares), then dig with a spade. 3 of the sites hold a unique god mjolnir/spear (Saradomin/Guthix/Zamorak) obtainable no other way besides trading; the rest give a spread of ores/runes/ammo. Not gated by combat.

name: The Eyes of Glouphrie

unlocks: Crystal saw seed — use on Brimstail's singing bowl or give to Ilfeen in Tirannwn for an enchanted crystal saw (28 charges, free recharge)

notes: Intermediate gnome-lore quest following on from The Grand Tree. Repair Oaknock's crystal-disc exchange machine (colour x shape-sides value puzzle), then use Hazelmere's mind-link (needs 46 Magic, not boostable) to relive a flashback of the Gnome/Goblin war revealing Glouphrie's illusions and Argento's death, then hunt down and kill 6 one-HP 'evil creature' spy-constructs hidden around the Stronghold and Grand Tree. Required for: The Path of Glouphrie, Monkey Madness II, Medium Western Provinces Diary.

name: The Feud

items: gloves that work for pickpocketing (not Barrows/ice/vambraces/Slayer)

notes: Must be able to kill a level 70 bandit champion and a level 75 tough guy (both safespottable). Buy a Kharidian headpiece and fake beard from Ali Morrisane and combine them into a desert disguise, then travel to Pollnivneach. Use 3 beers on Drunken Ali one at a time (talk through full dialogue between each) to get him to talk, then question bandits and Menaphite thugs. Buy two camels from Ali the Camel Man (500gp) and deliver receipts to a Menaphite and a bandit. Join Ali the Operator's gang, then complete three thieving tasks for him: pickpocket a villager (with street-urchin advice and later a blackjack knockout), infiltrate the mayor's house (disguise+gloves, sneak past guards, crack a 1-1-2-3-5-8 dial safe for jewels), and identify a traitor (buy special kebab sauce, use on a trough to check dung colour, get a snake charm/basket via the snake charmer's money pot, catch a desert snake, trade snake basket+dung to Ali the Hag for poison, spike a beer with it). Finally fight and kill the tough guy and bandit champion (each drops a blackjack tier or the scimitar), then report back to the mayor and to Ali Morrisane to finish.

name: The Final Dawn

items: Any bones (except sun-kissed) or raw/cooked meat

unlocks: Arkan blade
Lamp granting 55,000 xp to any combat skill 40+ (including Prayer)
Access to the Crypt of Tonali
Access to the Doom of Mokhaiotl (Osto-Ayak) 'delve' boss

notes: Master-tier Varlamore capstone. Requires both The Heart of Darkness and Perilous Moons, which each independently require Twilight's Promise -> Children of the Sun (shared ancestor, flattened to 4 unique quests). Infiltrate the Twilight Emissary cult undercover to steal a scroll (guarded by a hard-hitting Enforcer, lockpick puzzle), help Queen Zyanyi capture and interrogate cult leader Forebearer Janus in a safehouse heist, travel to Cam Torum to expose a bribed dwarf official (drink-barrel puzzle) and race the Tullus twins to an Old One keystone in Neypotzli (fight Lucius+Chimalli), defend the Crypt of Tonali through six cultist waves, defeat Ennius Tullus in a two-phase enrage boss fight, then solve chamber puzzles in the Tonali Cavern en route to Mokhaiotl.

name: The Forsaken Tower

unlocks: 6,000 coins

Ability to recolour the graceful outfit to Lovakengj colours

notes: Short intermediate quest: the Doors of Dinh, sealing away the Wintertodt, are failing. Retrieve Dinh's hammer from the Forsaken Tower to repair them by solving four puzzles — a water-jug coolant puzzle to safely light a furnace without taking damage, a power-grid alignment puzzle, a potion-riddle to cleanse a refinery without poisoning yourself, and a ring/disk-stacking pylon puzzle — then return the hammer to blacksmith Undor. Required for: A Kingdom Divided, Kourend & Kebos Medium Diary.

name: The Fremennik Trials

items: Raw shark or raw manta ray or raw sea turtle
Lyre (drop from Lanzig/Borrokar/Lensa/Freidir, or craft with Fletching 25 + Woodcutting 40 + Crafting 40)

unlocks: Boat access to Miscellania, Etceteria, Neitiznot, Jatizso + Rellekka facilities

Free Jarvald travel to Waterbirth Island
Fremennik clan name

notes: Requires beating level-69 Draugen and an unarmed/unarmoured fight vs Koschei the deathless (first 3 forms; 4th form is safe to "die" to, even on HCIM). Order: lose a drinking contest to Manni, get Hunters' Talisman from Sigli, talk Olaf, obtain a lyre (craft from swaying-tree branch via Lalli's stew quest-within-quest, or farm Lanzig's 1/17 drop). Spin golden wool in Seers' Village if crafting the lyre, buy low-alcohol keg from Poison Salesman. Back in Rellekka: use beer on council workman for strange object, enchant lyre with raw fish at a strange altar near the bridge, hunt the Draugen via talisman, light strange object and use on longhall pipe, play enchanted lyre on stage, then win the rematch drinking contest vs Manni with the low-alcohol keg swap. Sigmund/Swensen trial is a long NPC-chase-and-recall-order chain culminating in paying Askeladden 5,000 coins then repeating the NPC list in reverse order. Thorvald trial: bank everything (no weapons/armour/runes allowed), craft a dramen/celastrus staff underground, fight Koschei's first 3 forms unarmed. Peer the Seer trial: bank all bankable items, solve a riddle (each line gives one letter of a 4-letter word) then a multi-step item-transformation puzzle (bucket/jug/vase/disks/red herring) to unlock a trapdoor exit. Finish by reporting to Brundt. High spine value: gates Fremennik Isles (Neitiznot/Jatizso/Miscellania/Etceteria) sea access.

name: The Frozen Door

unlocks: Access to the Ancient Prison section of the God Wars Dungeon, including Nex

notes: After finishing Desert Treasure I, a messenger eventually appears and hands over a letter; reading (then destroying) it starts the miniquest. Kill each of the four God Wars Dungeon generals once to collect their frozen key piece: Kree'arra (armadyl, level 580), General Graardor (bandos, level 624), Commander Zilyana (saradomin, level 596), K'ril Tsutsaroth (zamorak, level 650) -- their respective bodyguards/lieutenants are listed as acceptable alternative kill-credit sources per the page's Details template. Combine all 4 pieces via 'assemble' into the frozen key, travel to the Frozen Door in the dungeon's southern part (east of Armadyl's Eyrie entrance), unlock it, and speak to Ashuelot Reis to finish and gain permanent access to the Ancient Prison/Nex.

name: The Garden of Death

items: Secateurs (obtainable in-quest, magic secateurs also work)

notes: Dialogue-free, environmental-storytelling 'Twisted Tale' quest. Translate a lost lizardman/Old Ones language by finding stone tablets, wood carvings and dirty notes across four hidden dungeons in sequence (Kebos campsite, Lake Molch Island, Xeric's Shrine, Ruins of Morra); each dungeon's translated words unlock the location clue for the next. Concludes revealing the Old Ones performed soul transference before the Citizens of Arceuus ever did. Wiki lists 'Required for completing: Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire' (Twisted Tale companion-quest cross-reference, not a normal prerequisite chain).

name: The General's Shadow

quests: Curse of the Empty Lord (for full ghostly robes)
Started Desert Treasure I (for the ring of visibility)
items: Ghostspeak amulet (Morytania legs will NOT substitute here)

unlocks: The Shadow sword (free-reclaimable from the ghost that spawns after the fight)

notes: Sequel to Fight Arena. Fully equip ghostly robes + ring of visibility + ghostspeak amulet, then talk to General Khazard's ghost south-east of Rellekka. He sends you to pay Sin Seer 40 coins in Seers' Village (upstairs in the spinning-wheel house), then relay word to 4 ghost scouts scattered across the map (south of Shantay Pass, south of the Gnome Stronghold entrance, between Falador and Draynor Manor, east of Tai Bwo Wannai) -- each just needs a conversation, no combat. Report back to Khazard, then enter the Goblin Cave east of the Fishing Guild carrying the severed leg, run to the north-east crack in the southern wall (a no-Prayer zone), and fight the level-160 ghost of Bouncer to 0 hp, which auto-throws the leg to finish. Auto-completes if the Fremennik or Desert league region is unlocked in a Leagues context.

name: The Giant Dwarf

items: Various ores/bars for consortium delivery tasks (obtainable in-quest with Smithing 50 or ~5000gp/Mining 55)

unlocks: Opens the Rise of the Red Axe storyline (Keldagrim quest series)
Membership in a chosen Keldagrim mining consortium company

notes: No formal quest prerequisites, but recreating King Alvis's battleaxe needs Thurgo (Mudskipper Point smith) which can be found either via The Knight's Sword or by asking the Keldagrim librarian then Reldo in Varrock library (no quest completion actually required). Introduces Keldagrim city. Flow: talk Dwarven Boatman, then Commander Veldaban, then Blasidar the sculptor (east Keldagrim) who needs exquisite clothes+boots+a repaired dwarven battleaxe for a statue. Clothes: get a costume book from a bookcase in the library (need <30kg weight to climb), buy coal/logs-spun cloth from Vermundi for 200gp. Boots: steal the left boot from Dromund when he's not looking, telegrab the right boot through his window. Axe: get it from Santiri's weapon shop, socket 3 sapphires, take to Thurgo at Mudskipper Point with an iron bar to repair (may need a redberry pie bribe). Deliver all 3 items to Riki (Blasidar's model) then talk Blasidar for a cutscene. Join the Consortium: pick one company (not Red Axe) upstairs in the market, run ore-delivery fetch-tasks for their secretary (hidden score, 20 points per success, need 100 to progress, wearing full H.A.M. robes gives Purple Pewter a +20 head start), then bar-delivery tasks for the director, then formally join the company and report back to Commander Veldaban to finish. Do not world-hop mid-consortium-grind or progress is lost.

name: The Golem

items: 4 soft clay (or bracelet of clay + pickaxe)
Phoenix feather (obtainable in-quest NW of ruins)

unlocks: 2 cut rubies + emeralds + sapphires (from the throne with hammer+chisel)
Magic carpet ride between Shantay Pass and Uzer

notes: Short desert quest. Read a letter near the broken golem, repair it with 4 soft clay, ask about the portal. Grab a black mushroom (grind for black dye) and a strange implement from the ruins basement (both reusable for Shadow of the Storm if doing that soon after). Talk to Elissa at the Digsite, read Varmen's notes in the Exam Centre bookcase, then talk to Curator Haig Halen at Varrock Museum about the Uzer statuette, pickpocket him for the display cabinet key, and take the statuette from an upstairs case. Return to Uzer, place the statuette in an empty alcove, rotate 4 statues to all face north to open the portal north; optionally chisel the throne for 2 each of sapphire/emerald/ruby. Make a phoenix quill pen (feather + black dye) and write the golem program on papyrus, then use the strange implement on the golem followed immediately by the program to finish. Region-opener with no known deeper dependency; sets up the desert quest line (Uzer).

name: The Grand Tree

items: 1,000 coins (only needed if you previously refused to help Femi at the Stronghold gate)

Spirit tree use in the Stronghold (after Tree Gnome Village)
