Black demon fightable in the Nightmare Zone

notes: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 172 Black demon (safespottable). Talk to King Shareen for a translation book and bark sample, deliver the sample to Hazelmere on the island east of Yanille (fairy ring CLS) for a message. Report back and tell the king about the man with the King's seal and the Daconia rocks; talk to imprisoned Charlie upstairs, search Glough's house cupboard for his journal, confront Glough (he arrests you), get the shipyard password 'Ka-Lu-Min' from Charlie, and escape via the gnome glider. On Karamja: run past level 53 jogres to the Ship Yard, give the password, get a lumber order from the Foreman (fight him or answer his security questions), and deliver the order to the king. Talk to Charlie again, get a key from Anita north-west of the tree, take invasion plans from Glough's chest, deliver them for 4 sticks, spell TUZO on the watchtower pillars in Glough's house, then descend to fight a level 172 Black demon (lure it against the daconia rocks north of the ladder for a safespot). Find the last daconia rock by searching roots, then finish by talking to the king. Required for: Monkey Madness I, The Eyes of Glouphrie.

name: The Great Brain Robbery

quests: Recipe for Disaster: Freeing Pirate Pete
Recipe for Disaster: Another Cook's Quest
items: Fishbowl helmet + diving apparatus, OR Medallion of the deep (underwater breathing)
10 wooden cats (tradeable, or craft yourself: 10 planks + 10 fur + a crafting table via player-owned house workshop or the GE)
4 oak planks, hammer, saw (only if building your own crafting table)
~100 nails (38 needed but bronze/iron may bend)
8 normal planks (18 if crafting the wooden cats yourself)
Ring of charos (re-obtainable in-quest by pickpocketing Fenkenstrain if lost)
reqs.reqs_note: also needs access to a POH workshop+crafting table or the GE, and the ability to kill a level-190 monster that disables Prayer (safespottable)

unlocks: Blessed lamp (5,000 xp in a chosen skill 30+, bankable)
Barrelchest anchor (repairable for 230,000 coins from 'Smith' at Mos Le'Harmless docks with a book o' piracy in inventory)
Prayer book
Harmony Island Teleport spell (Arceuus spellbook)
Ability to fight the Barrelchest in the Nightmare Zone

notes: Long quest chain flattened for prereqs: Cook's Assistant -> Recipe for Disaster subquests Freeing Pirate Pete + Another Cook's Quest; Priest in Peril -> Rum Deal; Big Chompy Bird Hunting + (Jungle Potion -> Druidic Ritual) -> Zogre Flesh Eaters; Rum Deal + Zogre Flesh Eaters -> Cabin Fever; Creature of Fenkenstrain + Cabin Fever are the direct gates. Walkthrough: talk to Brother Tranquility on Mos Le'Harmless (no pets), head north into the windmill, pull the statue and climb down, don underwater gear and dive, repair stairs, climb a ladder, peer through a peephole, then return to Brother Tranquility to be shuttled back and forth to Harmony Island. At Edgeville Monastery search the SW bookcase on the ground floor for a book, read it, then equip a Holy symbol back on Harmony Island and recite the prayer inside the windmill. At Dr Fenkenstrain's jail (NE of Canifis, via east/west stairs then a ladder) equip a Ring of charos and talk to him; talk to Rufus in Canifis food store repeatedly for a wolf whistle + crate part; make/buy 10 wooden cats, build a crate with a false bottom next to the doctor, fill it with cats, blow the whistle to summon Rufus, and give him the shipping order on the crate. Back at Harmony Island, dive to the shipwreck for a fuse, tinderbox, and keg; use the keg+fuse on the nearby monastery door and light it; inside, kill 4 Sorebones (level 57) for a cranial clamp, brain tongs, 3 bell jars, and 30 skull staples; deliver these to Fenkenstrain and talk to Brother Tranquility to prep the final fight. Instanced fight: confront Mi-Gor to trigger the Barrelchest (level 190) battle — each of his attacks disables Prayer, re-enabling it right after each hit avoids damage, or safespot from the monastery entrance by leaving and re-entering. Report the kill to Brother Tranquility to finish.

name: The Hand in the Sand

items: Redberries (buyable in-quest from the Port Sarim food store)
Bucket of sand (obtainable in-quest if you bring an empty bucket)

unlocks: Ability to buy pink dye from Betty
Bert will ship 84 buckets of sand to your bank once/day (UTC) if asked after the quest — also counts for an Ardougne Medium diary task
Access to the Wizards' Guild rune store

refs.title: The Hand in the Sand/Quick guide

notes: Talk to Bert in Yanille (house next to the POH portal) to get a sandy hand. Buy/bring a beer and give it to the Guard Captain at the Dragon Inn pub south of Bert's house. Ring the bell outside the Wizards' Guild (or enter with 66 Magic) and give the hand to Zavistic Rarve. Get Bert's rota from Bert, travel to Brimhaven, find Sandy south of the Shrimp and Parrot restaurant, search his desk for Sandy's rota, and pickpocket him for sand. Back in Yanille, get a magic scroll from Bert, ring the bell for a magical orb from Zavistic Rarve, who then offers to teleport you to Port Sarim. At Betty's magic shop get bottled water, mix in redberries then white berries (order matters) for pink dye, dye the lantern lens for a rose-tinted lens, then use the lens on the counter from the doorway for a truth serum, and hand over sand. In Brimhaven, distract Sandy with dialogue and use the truth serum on his coffee mug, then activate the magical orb and click through every dialogue option with him. Back in Yanille, ring the bell twice more at the Wizards' Guild (getting a cutscene on the second), sail to Entrana and talk to Mazion at the sandpit for a wizard's head, then return to Yanille and ring the bell/talk to Zavistic Rarve one last time to finish.

name: The Heart of Darkness

unlocks: Access to Tapoyauik

notes: Talk to Prince Itzla Arkan at the Teomat, then again after the cutscene. Travel to Quetzacalli Gorge, rent the pub's basement room from the bartender for 30 coins, rest on the bed in the SE room, talk to the Prince, then ask the general store shopkeeper for help and return to talk to the Prince again in the room. Head north through the gate to the Tower of Ascension, talk to the Prince outside, chat with recruits Nova/Sergius/Felius/Caritta, talk to the Prince again (no pets following), then enter the tower and talk to Forebearer Janus to climb up. First Trial: pickpocket a caged Emissary Ascended for a tower key, loot two chests for a book/paper via a word-decode puzzle unique per player, read a resulting poem, and speak the derived passcode to Forebearer Janus on the same floor (don't go downstairs mid-trial or it resets). Second Trial: a wave-based combat encounter where you must heal Prince Itzla with bandages from medical boxes while fighting melee/mage waves. Third Trial: question every cultist and accuse whichever one mentions 'Mictl' (heretical, not part of Emissary beliefs). Final Trial: fight Prince Itzla himself (level ~unlisted) — pray melee against basics, re-enable prayer immediately after his sword-swipe special (which disables it), and run behind him when he telegraphs his AOE special; he's immune to special attacks (unequip a Scythe of Vitur and punch/kick instead). Loot Twilight Emissary robes from a chest, equip them, bring the Prince through the temple east, then descend into the ruins south into an Ice Dungeon: mine through rocks, pull 4 levers scattered through icy rooms while dodging Frost Nagua/frost crabs/traps (each trap deals 10 damage), then pull a chain for future shortcut access. Statue Puzzle: collect 3 coloured icons from urns (level-104 Frost Nagua spawn continuously, Protect from Magic negates their hits), fix broken statues with the icons, then read corner murals to learn a player-specific activation order (Zema/Oma/Yeya/Naui = 1-4) and touch statues in that order to open the frozen door. Final Boss: fight Amoxliatl (level 263) praying magic, avoiding ice pools/spikes, re-enabling Protect from Magic after it's disabled, and meleeing the healing ice blocks she summons (or ignoring them at high level and just maging her). Finish by speaking to Servius, Teokan of Ralos, at the Sunrise Palace.

name: The Ides of Milk

unlocks: Access to Brutus, the cow boss
Cowbell amulet (from Gillie Groats)
Magic lamp: 1,000 xp in any combat skill, including Prayer (from Gillie Groats)

notes: Talk to Cassius by the Lumbridge pond to start. Talk to Gillie Groats then Seth Groats about their productive cows, search the shelves in Seth's farmhouse for 'The Groats principles' book, and bring it to Cassius for a milk sample. Drink the sample in front of Cassius to get a second sample for Duke Horacio; deliver it, then talk to Gillie Groats and drink the sample in front of her for a permit to deal with the bull. Open the pen in the NE corner of the cow farm and fight the bull (recommended 15+ Combat), then report back to Gillie Groats and Cassius, and finally talk to Gillie Groats again to collect the rewards.

name: The Knight's Sword

items: 2 or 4 iron bars (4 if you want your own extra blurite sword)
1-3 blurite ore (mined in-quest — extra for a spare sword and/or the Falador easy diary)
Any pickaxe (one spawns free in Rimmington)

unlocks: Blurite sword (only if you brought extra ore/bars for a personal copy)
Ability to smelt blurite ore into blurite bars (members only)

notes: Talk to the Squire in the White Knights' Castle courtyard about his lost ceremonial sword. Talk to Reldo in the Varrock Palace library about the Imcando dwarves (skip if Thurgo was already met via The Giant Dwarf). Find Thurgo south of Port Sarim near the anvil (north of fairy ring AIQ) and give him a redberry pie, talk to him again, then return to the Squire. Climb to the White Knights' Castle 1st floor, go west and up the east staircase, and search the cupboard in Sir Vyvin's bedroom for a portrait while he isn't looking. Bring the portrait (and iron bars) to Thurgo. Enter the Asgarnian Ice Dungeon via the trapdoor east of Thurgo, follow the path west/north/east, and mine blurite ore along the southern wall of the icy area (mine a second if doing the Falador easy diary). Give Thurgo the iron bars and blurite ore to forge the replacement sword, then deliver the sword to the Squire to finish.

name: The Lost Tribe

items: Light source (candle/torch/oil lamp — carry a tinderbox too, since going off-route near swamp gas can extinguish it and risks an explosion)

unlocks: Goblin Bow and Goblin Salute emotes
Access to the Dorgesh-Kaan mine
A ring of life
Access to Nardok's Bone Weapons store (Dorgeshuun crossbow, bone bolts, etc.)
A mining helmet, from returning the brooch to Mistag

notes: Talk to Sigmund on Lumbridge Castle's 1st floor to start. Ask around (Duke Horacio, the Cook, Hans, Father Aereck, Bob, Gee/Donie) about the collapsed cellar wall until someone mentions a goblin sighting, then report back to the Duke. Climb down the kitchen trapdoor, mine through rubble in the basement, light your light source, and grab a brooch about 10 steps into the cave; show it to the Duke. In Varrock Palace library, talk to Reldo and search the northern bookcase on the west wall for a goblin symbol book; read it. At Goblin Village, talk to the generals and mediate between them until you learn the Goblin Bow and Goblin Salute emotes. Return to the Lumbridge cellar caves and follow the mapped path (clicking the minimap auto-paths it) to reach Mistag; perform the Goblin Bow for him, then let him guide you back to Lumbridge. Report to the Duke (he won't believe you), then pickpocket Sigmund and unlock his chest in the other room for HAM robes (keep for Death to the Dorgeshuun). Head to the HAM lair west of Lumbridge Castle, pick the trapdoor lock, enter, and search a crate south of the ladder for stolen silverware. Return it to the Duke, who fires Sigmund and hands you a signed peace treaty. Go back to the cellar, follow Kazgar down to Mistag, and give him the treaty to trigger the finishing cutscene.

name: The Path of Glouphrie

items: Tree Gnome Village Dungeon key (unless Roving Elves has been started; obtainable in-quest)

rewards.xp: 4 magic lamps: Slayer 20,000, Thieving 5,000, Strength 30,000, Magic 5,000
unlocks: Access to the Poison Waste Dungeon
Warped creatures unlockable as a Slayer task (drop warped sceptre)
New spirit tree destination in the Poison Waste

notes: Experienced-tier sequel to The Eyes of Glouphrie. Expose King Bolren's new pet 'Dumpling' as another evil creature via Yewnock's value-matching disc machine, found through a monolith-pushing puzzle in the Tree Gnome Village Dungeon and Bolrie's diary cutscenes recounting gnome political history. Track down explorer Longramble in the Galarpos Mountains and heal the corrupted spirit tree Incomitatus with a crystal chime (unlocking it as a teleport), then race through the Warped Depths dungeon past aggressive Warped Terrorbirds and kill three of them before Hazelmere teleports you to safety from a tar-and-gas trap. Required for: While Guthix Sleeps.

name: The Queen of Thieves

items: Stew (ingredients purchasable in-quest for 6 coins, requires 25 Cooking)

unlocks: 2,000 coins
Kharedst's memoirs page 'The fisher's flute' (teleport centre of Port Piscarilius)
Ability to recolour the graceful outfit to Piscarilius colours

notes: Intermediate quest investigating Port Piscarilius's uniting gang, the Saviours of Kourend. Bribe an informant with cooked stew to learn their hideout in The Warrens, fake-kill a rival gang leader to prove loyalty, then confront the titular Queen of Thieves — revealed to be Lady Shauna Piscarilius, using the gang to target the corrupt rather than terrorise the poor — and burgle a Council member's house for evidence tying him to the Disciples of Yama. Required for: A Kingdom Divided, Kourend & Kebos Medium Diary.

name: The Red Reef

items: Cannons on the boat for ship combat, or magic/ranged gear to skip it

unlocks: Partial access to Red Rock (unlock as a Sailor's amulet teleport destination via the Sailors' Marker by the bank)
Access to the Sacred Grove
Bosun's workbench schematic

notes: Experienced-tier Sailing quest sequel to Troubled Tortugans. Investigate the Red Reef Trading Company on injured navigator Floopa's behalf: sink or crew-kill two pirate ships at sea, then fight through six pirates and captain Black Eye Bethel (3-stage weapon upgrades) at Last Light, help company founder Spencer Brentwood repair underwater coral dredgers (fighting a safespottable giant lobster), and learn the company's coral shortage is driving their conflict with the tortugans over the Little Pearl.

name: The Restless Ghost

unlocks: Ghostspeak amulet

notes: Novice quest; only real gate is being able to kill or flee a level 13 skeleton. Talk to Father Aereck about the haunted Lumbridge graveyard, head to Father Urhney's hut west of Lumbridge Swamp and talk to him, then go to the small room in the graveyard, equip the Ghostspeak amulet and talk to the coffin-ghost. Go to the Wizards' Tower basement and search the altar for a ghost's skull (a level 13 skeleton spawns but does not need to be killed, just leave), then use the skull on the open coffin to finish. Required for: Animal Magnetism, Curse of the Empty Lord, Ghosts Ahoy, Making History, Nature Spirit, Creature of Fenkenstrain.

name: The Tourist Trap

items: Desert shirt, robe, and boots (non-black versions only — all buyable from Shantay at Shantay Pass)
1 bronze bar (bring 2-3 extra; 24-96% smith success chance depending on Smithing level)
A way into the desert: Shantay Pass, desert amulet 2, teleport tablets/scrolls, fairy rings, or a pharaoh's sceptre

unlocks: Choice of 4,650 xp split across two picks (same skill twice allowed) among Agility/Fletching/Smithing/Thieving
Ability to smith dart tips
Wrought iron key — opens a special door in the Desert Mining Camp with mithril/adamantite ore inside
Full slave robes
6 bronze darts
Ability to hire Ex-Captain Siad as a ship crewmate (needs 50 Sailing)

notes: Talk to Irena south of Shantay Pass to start. At the Desert Mining Camp entrance, goad the Mercenary Captain (level 47, safespottable/cannonable) into attacking via dialogue and kill him for the gate key — do not skip the dialogue or enter without full waterskins, both can get you jailed or killed by heat damage. Unequip all weapons/armour before entering the gate (graceful is fine if not worn over the required desert clothing) — equipping gear or removing the disguise inside gets you jailed. Trade with the male slave on the east wall for slave clothes when no guard is watching (getting caught = jail; escape by bending the window bars, climbing out over the wall/cliff, and re-entering the gate), equip the clothes, and enter the mine north. Follow the path to a guarded cave entrance and talk to a guard until he mentions a Tenti pineapple, then leave the mine, fully unequip the slave robes, and exit camp via the front gate (or fairy ring BIQ) — leaving in disguise through either the gate or the mine door also risks jail. At Bedabin Camp (west of the mining camp) talk to Al Shabim in the south tent for a Bedabin key. Back in the mining camp (slave outfit on again), climb to the jail building's upper floor, search the southernmost west-wall bookcase for a sailing-books clue, then talk to Captain Siad in the same room and open a chest for technical plans. Show the plans to Al Shabim, then use the northern tent's anvil to smith a bronze dart tip from a bar and fletch it with 10 feathers into a prototype dart; trade it to Al Shabim for the Tenti pineapple. Re-suit in slave clothes, give the pineapple to a mine guard, head east past a mine cart, search barrels for a spare barrel, then search the mine cart to trigger a cutscene ride (redo if it doesn't trigger). Do not talk to Ana — instead follow the west-north-west path and use the spare barrel on her (don't try to teleport with her in your inventory, it drops the barrel). Ride the cart back, put Ana in it then get in yourself, search barrels to find her again, place her on the north-west winch bucket, decline the guard's suspicion-raising offer of help, then surface, operate the winch (south-west of camp), search the barrel west of the winch for Ana, load her onto the wooden cart at camp centre, and talk your way past the mine cart driver. Board the cart and return to Irena for the reward. Downstream note: required for starting Desert Treasure I.

name: Tree Gnome Village

items: 6 normal logs (or an axe to cut goblin drops in the maze, or from a stored canoe)

unlocks: Spirit tree transport network
Gnome amulet
Khazard warlord fightable in the Nightmare Zone

notes: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 112 Khazard warlord (safespottable). Talk to King Bolren (teleports you to the north-west corner of the maze), give Commander Montai 6 logs on the battlefield's south-west edge, then agree to help further. Talk to all 3 tracker gnomes around the Khazard Stronghold to get 4-digit ballista coordinates from riddles, then fire the south-west ballista with that code. Climb into the stronghold over the crumbled wall, ignore or kill the level 48 Khazard commanders, take an orb of protection from the upstairs chest, exit, and report to Elkoy to be sent to the village centre. Track down and kill the level 112 Khazard warlord north-west of the battlefield (safespottable near the West Ardougne wall) for the other two orbs, then return all 3 orbs to King Bolren to finish. Required for: Monkey Madness I, The Path of Glouphrie, Medium Western Provinces Diary, Medium Varrock Diary.

name: Troll Stronghold

items: Climbing boots or 12 coins to buy them

unlocks: Law talisman (rebuyable from Dunstan for 1,000gp, or obtainable via Guardians of the Rift)
Ability to make law runes
Access to Trollheim, the Troll Stronghold, and the Law Altar
Access to the God Wars Dungeon
Dad fightable in the Nightmare Zone

notes: Intermediate quest requiring defeat of a level 113 Troll General (safespottable). Talk to Denulth south of the Burthorpe teleport, follow the path to the stronghold buying climbing boots from Tenzing along the way, then fight Dad in the arena (safespottable from the gate) -- the fight ends automatically before he dies. Exit north, get past Thrower trolls (sprint, protect from missiles, or agility shortcuts), and enter the stronghold. Inside: kill a Troll General for a prison key (safespottable), pickpocket (30+ Thieving) or kill sleeping Twig and Berry to free both prisoners, escape via games necklace or the secret exit, and talk to Dunstan to finish. Required for: Desert Treasure I, Devious Minds, Eadgar's Ruse, Monkey Madness II, Troll Romance; 15 Agility plus defeating Dad also gates God Wars Dungeon entry (without Ghommal's hilt).

name: Troubled Tortugans

unlocks: Access to the Great Conch and its facilities (fairy ring code CJQ, notice board)
Ability to fight gryphons

notes: Bandage and rescue the injured tortugan Floopa (seaweed + palm leaf from a shaken palm tree = bandage); she boards your boat and directs you to the Great Conch. Speak to Elder Raley in the village for a repair list, then gather materials around town (saw, hammer, 6 tortugan scutes, 10 jatoba logs chopped from jatoba trees, 6 sea shells on the beach) and fix six structures (Elder Krill's stall + store wall, Elder Strom's store wall + crate, Elder Coco's stall + crate). Track a gryphon from footprints at the market's central monument, following the trail through inspectable objects (flowers, rockslides, plants) to a cave; clear roots with an axe and kill the level-95 gryphon inside. Elder Blunn then gives a Tortugan shield (cape slot, must stay equipped) before sailing to The Little Pearl to fight the level-235 Shellbane Gryphon — use Protect from Melee/Ranged, ranged/magic negates a mechanic, its charge knockback (up to 65 dmg) is blocked by standing near a tree/eggs, step off tiles when whirlwinds spawn on wing-flap warning. After the fight, talk to Korel about a mysterious ship before returning to Elder Raley to finish.

name: Twilight's Promise

items: Gear for two combat styles, or a poisoned weapon (slower)

unlocks: Civitas illa Fortis Teleport spell
Quetzal Transport System

notes: Intermediate quest, the proper introduction to Varlamore's capital. Help Prince Itzla investigate a plot against his father Servius by rounding up six Knights of Varlamore scattered around Civitas illa Fortis — a pickpocket hunt in the bazaar, a smuggling-crate search on the docks, a sparring fight against knight Mezan, and reuniting drunk Azali with her partner. A forged letter gets an innocent knight killed by Ennius Tullus; learn of the doomsday cult Twilight Emissaries and their belief in the Final Dawn, then fight off 8 cultists at the Teomat as Servius is mysteriously wounded despite no cultist reaching him. Required for: Perilous Moons, The Heart of Darkness.

name: Underground Pass

unlocks: Iban's staff + Iban Blast spell
Klank's gauntlets

notes: One-way point of no return dungeon crawl west of Ardougne toward Tirannwn. Talk King Lathas then Koftik at West Ardougne cave entrance, enter dungeon (no going back except via lever/rope pull). Light an arrow via Koftik's cloth+tinderbox, shoot the bridge guide-rope, cross grid maze carefully (keep HP>15), pull lever. Collect 4 light orbs (avoid wall traps, use plank on flat rocks, disarm trap on 4th), fuel furnace, descend well. Navigate slave-cage/pipe area (50 Thieving shortcut optional). Get unicorn horn via railing-on-boulder trick, kill 3 paladins (safespottable) for badges, use badges+horn on well to open door. Meet dwarves Niloof and Klank (get gauntlets - keep inv slot free), steal Witch's cat to enter her house for loot, kill 3 demons for amulets, brew dwarf beer + tinderbox on tomb. Kill Kalrag the spider (safespottable, ignores Protect Melee), get Iban's dove wearing Klank's gauntlets. Kill a Disciple of Iban for ceremonial robes (required to enter temple), use Doll of Iban on the well in Iban's temple (repeat through his attacks, ~8dmg unblockable spikes) to destroy him. Return to King Lathas via Koftik. Gates access to Tirannwn/Elf lands questline infrastructure.

name: Vale Totems (miniquest)

items: Oak logs or better (obtainable during the quest by chopping nearby trees with an axe)
Four fletched items of the same wood type as the logs, e.g. unstrung longbows or shields (obtainable during the quest)

unlocks: Sacraments of ent folk
Access to the Vale Totems Fletching minigame

notes: Introductory miniquest gating the Vale Totems Fletching minigame. Talk to Ranulph, who shows you a totem the local ents damaged; child NPC Isadora tells you to rebuild it. Chop a nearby tree for oak-or-better logs, click the totem site to lay the new base, then return to Ranulph/Isadora. Next, click the totem and select carving options for a set of nearby spirit animals (randomized per player) to decorate it. Return again, then fletch 4 items (bows/shields) from the same wood as the totem and use them on the totem to finish decorating it. The ents leave an offering to collect, then speak to either NPC to receive Sacraments of ent folk and complete the miniquest, unlocking the minigame proper. No combat.

name: Vampyre Slayer

items: Hammer (1 coin, general store north of Blue Moon Inn)

unlocks: Count Draynor added to Nightmare Zone

notes: Very short early quest, beats level-34 Count Draynor (safespottable, high healing - garlic weakens him and is strongly recommended). Talk Morgan, grab garlic from his upstairs cupboard. Travel to the Blue Moon Inn in Varrock, buy a beer, talk Dr Harlow twice for a stake, buy a hammer from the general store just north of the inn. Head to Draynor Manor basement (east stairs, not the west room), open the coffin and kill Count Draynor with combat gear (hammer/stake just need to be in inventory, not actively used). Free F2P quest, low reqs, no known deeper dependency chain.

name: Watchtower

items: Death rune and light source (obtainable in-quest)

unlocks: Watchtower Teleport (scroll)
Access to Gu'Tanoth ogre city and Ogre Enclave (blue dragon slaying spot)
5,000 coins
"Friend of the Ogres" ogre reputation

notes: Must be able to beat level-68 Gorad (safespottable) and run past level-111 blue dragons. Flow: talk to Wizard in tower, get fingernails from bush below, talk to Og NW of Yanille for Toban's key, rope across to Jangerberry island (talk Grew), enter Tunnel to Toban south of Gu'Tanoth, kill Gorad for tooth, give Toban dragon bones for relic part 3, loot chest, return relics to Wizard for ogre relic. Enter Gu'Tanoth, steal rock cake from market, pay 20 coins/talk guards to reach Skavid caves, get skavid map (death rune), solve 4-cave skavid word-repeat puzzle, gold bar unlocks east gate, second nightshade in Mad Skavid cave, use nightshade on Enclave guard to trigger cutscene. Brew magic ogre potion (guam unf + jangerberries + ground bat bones, in that order) with Wizard. Return to Enclave, potion 6 ogre shamans for crystal 3, mine Rock of Dalgroth for crystal 4, return all 4 crystals to correct pillar colors (NE magenta/SE grey/SW yellow/NW cyan), pull lever. Key spine value: unlocks Ogre Enclave blue dragons + Watchtower teleport infrastructure.

name: Waterfall Quest

unlocks: 2 diamonds
2 gold bars
40 mithril seeds

notes: Intermediate quest requiring the ability to withstand/evade level 86 Fire Giants and a level 84 Moss Guardian with no equipment (Glarial's Tomb bans gear). Talk to Almera, raft to the island, talk to Hudon then Hadley, find a Book on Baxtorian in an upstairs bookcase and read it. Enter the Tree Gnome Village maze/dungeon (worth starting Tree Gnome Village here too), find a key in a crate, unlock the west gate, and get Glarial's pebble from Golrie (keep it banked for Roving Elves later). Bank everything except the pebble, food, and a teleport method, use the pebble on Glarial's Tombstone east of the Waterfall to enter, and retrieve Glarial's amulet and urn from the tomb. Return with rope + 6 air/water/earth runes + amulet + urn: cross the raft/rope route into the waterfall dungeon, unlock the door past the Fire Giants, place one of each rune on 3 pillars, wear the amulet at Glarial's statue to raise the ground, then pour the urn into the chalice to finish. Required for: Desert Treasure I, Legends' Quest, Medium Kandarin Diary, Roving Elves, The Path of Glouphrie.

name: While Guthix Sleeps

skills: Warriors' Guild access (Attack+Strength >=130, or 99 Attack, or 99 Strength)

unlocks: Access to the Ancient Guthixian Temple and tormented demons
Duradel's notes (via Kuradal) — one-time Slayer xp = 15x current Slayer level, plus ability to craft emberlight/scorching bow/purging staff
Loot from Movario's base: 100 death runes, 100 fire runes, 100 coal, 100 magic logs
Elite black armour and Dark Squall's robes
Access to the Black Knight Catacombs
Adoptable broav pet if kept

notes: Grandmaster Mahjarrat-storyline quest, sequel to Defender of Varrock, chasing the Stone of Jas. Thaerisk sends you to kill 2 assassins, then investigate Movario at the Fight Arena: bribe/charm the Khazard Launderer for info, train a broav at Feldip Hills to sniff out Movario's hideout, then break into his underground base solving a rune-symbol door puzzle, a bookcase-lever/wire maze, a trapped bed chest, and a weight/pressure-gauge puzzle to recover two volumes of notes. Rally allies: rescue Idria from mercenaries at McGrubor's Wood, plant a teleorb on a shady stranger near Draynor for Akrisae, get a snapdragon seed from Thaerisk and enrich it with pink dye + Betty's help, plant it at Falador castle. Use NPC Contact to summon Turael/Mazchna/Cyrisus/Duradel about Lucien, harvest the enriched snapdragon for a truth serum to interrogate a captured shady stranger, then recruit Ghommal/Harrallak/Sloane at the Warriors' Guild. Infiltrate Black Knights' Fortress in disguise, descend into the Black Knight Catacombs, farm elite black armour off Elite Black Knights, rescue Silif and swap armour with him, then face Surok Magis (magic-only, 450hp, Vorkath-style attack rotation: bind/weaken his summoned warriors, telegrab-and-alch his explosive orbs, counter his elemental surges with the opposite element) atop the fortress. Teleport to Lucien's camp via a strange teleorb, then descend the Chasm of Tears: collect 4 elemental orbs from braziers, solve elemental-block puzzles inside skull tunnels, and match herblore-ingredient offerings to 8 of 13 statues (offerings vary per player) to open the final door. Finish with the Balance Elemental (524, prayer-switches by its attack style, drains stats every 3-4 hits, doubles up at 25% hp) then two Tormented Demons alongside the Guardians of Armadyl (can be cheesed by letting the Guardians finish them off from behind a pillar) before returning to Idria to complete.

name: Witch's House

skills: Ability to defeat 4 monsters (levels 19-53) back-to-back with no break

unlocks: Witch's experiment (all four forms) accessible as a Nightmare Zone boss

notes: A boy's ball landed in a scary witch's garden. Sneak into her house (key under the potted plant by the front door), grab gloves and a magnet from the basement, then trap a mouse with cheese and the magnet. Cross the garden to the fountain and shed without the Witch seeing you (stick to hedges, never walk on grass, or you must repeat the break-in); getting caught mid-fight also drops your combat stats if you grab the ball too early. Open the shed and fight the Witch's experiment through all four escalating forms (levels 19/30/42/53, corners of the shed are safespottable), take the ball, then sneak/teleport back out and return it to the boy.

name: Witch's Potion
notes: Hetty the witch wants help brewing a potion. Gather four ingredients: kill a rat in the archery shop west of Hetty for a rat's tail, buy an eye of newt from Betty (3gp), kill a giant rat at the chapel south of Port Sarim for raw rat meat and burn it on Hetty's fireplace, and pick an onion from the field north of Rimmington. Bring all four to Hetty and drink from her cauldron to finish.

name: X Marks the Spot

unlocks: 200 coins
antique lamp (300xp, any skill, reclaimable if lost)
ability to receive scroll boxes
beginner scroll box (if not already owned)
+1 cap on all tiers of clue scrolls

notes: No requirements, tutorial-tier treasure hunt intro quest. Talk to Veos in the Sheared Ram pub, Lumbridge, to start. Dig at 4 marked spots in sequence: by Bob's Brilliant Axes (west of the plant against the wall), behind Lumbridge Castle near the kitchen door, north-west of the Draynor Village jail by the wheat farm, and in the pig pen next to Martin the Master Gardener. Use the resulting ancient casket on Veos at the Port Sarim docks to finish.

name: Zogre Flesh Eaters

skills: Ability to defeat a level 111 enemy

unlocks: 3 ourg bones + 2 zogre bones
Ability to make Relicym's balm
Ability to fletch comp ogre bows and brutal arrows
Ability to wear inoculation bracelets
Access to Uglug's shop (give Uglug a dose of Relicym's balm)
Bonus: sell the black prism to Zavistic Rarve (2,000gp) or Yanni Salika in Shilo Village (5,000gp)

notes: Grish asks you to investigate zombified ogres (zogres) infesting the ogre ceremonial grounds. Head into the cave below Jiggig, being careful of disease from zogre/skogre attacks if under 89 combat (carry super restores). Loot a ruined backpack and a torn page from a skeleton/lectern, then pry open a coffin with a knife for a black prism. Head to Yanille, use the recovered Dragon Inn tankard on the bartender there, then visit Zavistic Rarve at the Wizards' Guild. Investigate the ogres' bedridden neighbor Sithik Ints: search his room for a portraiture book, necromancy book, papyrus/charcoal and a Book of 'H.A.M.', read and use all three on him, then sketch a portrait that reads as an honest "classic realist charcoal portrait" (higher Crafting speeds this up) and get it signed by the Dragon Inn bartender. Bring the necromancy book, H.A.M. book, tankard and signed portrait to Zavistic Rarve for a strange potion; spike Sithik's tea with it, then question him for the secrets to Relicym's balm, curing the disease, and removing the undead ogres. Return to Grish for an ogre gate key and to learn comp ogre bow use, then fight the level-111 Slash Bash (uses Ranged+Melee — safespot with Protect from Missiles, or use Crumble Undead) in the ceremonial chamber to finish.

name: Wintertodt

rewards.xp: Firemaking (level*100) on subdue with 500+ points, plus per-action xp during the fight: lighting braziers (FM 6x), cutting/fletching bruma roots (WC 0.3x / Fletching 0.6x), feeding roots/kindling (FM 3x/3.8x), fixing broken braziers (Construction 4x, needs a POH), brewing rejuvenation potions (Herblore 0.1x)
rewards.items: Reward Cart loot scaled to the searcher's skill levels (higher Herblore/Mining/Fishing/Crafting/Farming/Woodcutting = rarer pulls); every 500 points guarantees +1 extra reward beyond the base 2

notes: Low-req AFK-ish Firemaking trainer that also feeds Woodcutting/Fletching/Construction/Herblore xp as side effects of the same rotation -- a good early-game multi-skill xp source once FM 50 is hit, and the reward cart is a steady supply chain (herbs/ore/fish/gems/seeds/logs) gated by the player's OTHER skill levels, so it stays worth revisiting as those skills climb. Construction xp specifically requires owning a POH (brazier repair). Entry is cheap: no quest required, only FM 50 and basic tools/potions obtainable inside the minigame itself.

name: Tempoross

items: harpoon or barb-tail harpoon (skippable w/ barbarian fishing)
small or big fishing net (only needed to search the reward pool)

rewards.xp: Fishing xp gained continuously during the fight itself (no lump subdue bonus, unlike Wintertodt) -- rate scales up through 'Not cooking' vs 'Cooking' play styles per the page's xp-rate section
rewards.items: Reward pool permits (1 per 2000 pts, +1 per further 700) spent on: spirit flakes (bonus-catch buff), casket, soaked pages, fish barrel, tackle box, big harpoonfish, Tome of water (empty, needs Magic 50 to wield, infinite water runes + spell dmg/accuracy boost), dragon harpoon, Tiny tempor pet

notes: Zero entry requirements -- no combat or skill level gates participation, only base Fishing level affects reward-pool loot quality at the time of searching (skill boosts don't count). Best pure Fishing trainer with no subdue bonus (all xp is per-action during the fight), and the reward pool is the only source of the Tome of water (magic dmg/accuracy utility item) and the fish barrel/tackle box QoL storage items. Good round-robin activity since it has no gear/skill investment barrier -- can be done from level 1 in every relevant skill.

name: Guardians of the Rift

rewards.xp: Runecraft level x 45 per game on reaching 300+ total energy gathered (elemental+catalytic combined); up to 1200 energy/game (1320 with abyssal lantern burning yew logs), capped 1000 per energy type
rewards.items: Elemental/catalytic talismans (altar access), Abyssal needle (merges essence pouches into a colossal pouch, needs Crafting 56), Abyssal lantern, Abyssal pearls (currency for Temple Supplies: raiments of the eye +60% runes full set, ring of the elements, Blood talisman exclusive here), intricate pouch (runes/dragon items/hard clue chance), Abyssal protector pet
rewards.unlocks: Ring of the elements teleport network; raiments of the eye passive Runecrafting rune-multiplier set

notes: Primary high-level Runecraft trainer/gold-and-gear source once Temple of the Eye is done -- gates on RC 27 (trivially met via the quest's own xp) not a high skill investment, so it's accessible early but scales its xp reward with current RC level, making it worth returning to repeatedly as RC climbs. The exclusive Blood talisman and the raiments' passive rune-multiplier set make it the natural feeder loop for pushing RC further (buy talisman access -> craft more runes -> more RC xp elsewhere).

name: Barbarian Assault

rewards.items: Honour points -> Penance armour set (fighter hat/torso/skirt/gloves + runner boots, each ~2100 pts across all 4 roles + 95,000gp + 7 Penance Queen kills for the granite body); fighter torso is the headline item (Strength bonus, no stat reqs to wear); gambling honour points can yield dragon chainbody or the Penance Queen pet (1/1000 on high gamble)
rewards.unlocks: Elite Kandarin Diary requires level-5 upgrades in all 4 roles (5,600 honour points total, capped 5000/role)

notes: 5-player team minigame (attacker/defender/healer/collector roles, 10 waves, whole team restarts a wave on any death/leave) gated by nothing beyond forming a team and completing Captain Cain's tutorial -- no skill or quest requirement. Its main progression value is the fighter torso (cheap, no-stat-req strength-bonus body slot, a long-lived early/mid melee upgrade) and being a hard requirement for the Elite Kandarin Diary (all 4 roles to level 5). Points-gambling is also a source of the dragon chainbody and the rare Penance Queen pet.

name: Pest Control

reqs.combat_level: 40/70/100 for Novice/Intermediate/Veteran lander respectively -- the only entry gate

rewards.xp: Commendation points can be exchanged for Combat, Crafting, and Woodcutting xp
rewards.items: Void Knight equipment (regular + elite void, ~850-1910 commendation points per piece/set), plus coins per win (10x current combat level); commendation points capped at 4000 held at once

notes: Pure combat-level gated (40/70/100, no skill/quest reqs) 5-25 player wave-defense minigame; must keep an 'activity bar' above 0% to earn points, and points cannot be earned while Dwarf multicannon or Alchemy spells are used. Its main progression payoff is Void Knight armour, a cheap universal combat-bonus set (no stat requirements to wear) usable across melee/ranged/magic, making it a strong low-investment gear upgrade for any account, plus a modest side supply of Crafting/Woodcutting xp via point exchange.

name: Blast Furnace

skills.smithing: none to enter; 60 waives the 2500c/10min entry fee and unlocks the nearby anvil

quests: The Giant Dwarf (started only, for Keldagrim access)
items: ice gloves, smiths gloves (i), or a bucket of water (fast bar-cooling)
coal bag / goldsmith gauntlets recommended for inventory efficiency

rewards.xp: Full Smithing xp per bar smelted at greatly boosted bars/hour vs a normal furnace -- e.g. steel bar 2,900-3,400 bars/h (50,800-95,400 xp/h with a coal bag), mithril up to 3,700 bars/h (up to 111,000 xp/h); scales by bar tier

notes: The fastest bar-smelting method in the game (steel/mithril+ especially) -- entry is cheap (2500c/10min, waivable at Smithing 60) but running it on a Blast Furnace themed world costs 72,000c/hour into a shared coffer to pay dwarven workers who auto-operate the furnace; on non-themed worlds it must be run manually (slower, solo). This is the standard high-volume Smithing xp/bar-production venue once a player has Keldagrim access via starting The Giant Dwarf, and its steel/mithril+ bar output feeds directly into cannonball/weapon-armour smithing chains elsewhere in progression.

name: Nightmare Zone

quests: any 5 of a long list including The Grand Tree, Dragon Slayer I, Monkey Madness I, Desert Treasure I, Lost City, Tree Gnome Village, Vampyre Slayer, What Lies Below, etc -- only 5 completions needed, not all

rewards.items: NMZ reward points spent on: Herblore-ingredient/rune resources (flax, sand, seaweed, runes, herb boxes capped 15/day, scrolls of redirection -- ironmen restricted to scrolls-only on this tab), combat-stat upgrades, and minigame-exclusive potions unusable outside NMZ (overload, absorption, super ranging/magic) stored in on-site barrels and drinkable without delaying attacks

notes: Gate is shallow (any 5 completed quests from a long list, not a specific set) making it reachable early despite the list containing some mid-game quests. Historically the go-to AFK melee/ranged/magic combat trainer via the boss-rotation 'dream' with cannon/auto-retaliate, though the wiki page itself doesn't state raw xp/hr here (see Bosses/Playing sections if xp numbers are needed downstream). Its progression value is largely as a combat xp treadmill plus a cheap secondary Herblore-ingredient/rune shop and the only source of Overload/Absorption potions, which are strong utility for other high-level PvM.

name: Pyramid Plunder

skills.thieving: 21 minimum to start; every +10 (unboostable) opens the next tier of rooms up to 91

quests: Icthlarin's Little Helper (started only, for Sophanem access)

rewards.items: Artefacts (urns/chests/sarcophagi) sellable for 50-1250gp each or used to recharge a Pharaoh's sceptre; the sceptre itself is a rare drop from golden chests/sarcophagi in later rooms, rate improving from 1/4200 at Thieving 21-30 up to ~1/63.5 cumulative at 91+ per full run
rewards.unlocks: Pharaoh's sceptre (Sophanem/Kalphite Cave/Menaphos/Nardah/Sophanem-Pyramid teleport network, useable as this minigame's own fast-entry item once charged)

notes: Thieving-only gate (21 to start, no quest requirement beyond starting Icthlarin's Little Helper for city access), with room tiers unlocked by unboostable Thieving levels up to 91 -- so it scales naturally as a Thieving-training destination through the mid-to-late game rather than being a one-time unlock. Primary progression payoff is the Pharaoh's sceptre, a teleport/utility item recharged by the very artefacts this minigame produces, making it a self-sustaining loop once obtained.

name: Volcanic Mine

items: 30 numulite per entry (or 3,000 numulite one-time for permanent access)

rewards.xp: Mining xp scales with level: ~40,000 xp/h at Mining 50 (~7,200 pts), rising to 85,000-95,000 xp/h at Mining 99 (~15,400-17,200 pts) with good pickaxe + play; roughly 3.8 Mining xp granted per point at game end (3.895 with prospector kit), plus xp earned live during the game
rewards.items: Ore fragments (kept even if not cashed for points) plus calcite/pyrophosphite/Fossil Island fossils/numulite gathered along the way; points spent at Petrified Pete's Ore Shop, with runite ore fragments (unlocked Mining 85) worth the most per point

notes: High-level (Mining 50+) high-intensity Mining trainer/moneymaker on Fossil Island -- dying or teleporting out forfeits all fragments/points for that run, so it's a riskier, more active alternative to AFK Mining spots. Runite-fragment access at Mining 85 makes it scale further into the late game, and it doubles as a numulite/fossil income source useful for other Fossil Island unlocks (Fossil Storage, Ore Shop purchases).

name: Motherlode Mine

skills.mining: 30 (unboostable) to mine ore veins; no level required merely to enter; upper floor needs Mining 57 + 100 golden nuggets one-time

rewards.xp: 60 Mining xp per ore vein mined (pay-dirt), continuous low-intensity training; golden nuggets drop 3.13% per pay-dirt cleaned (~1 every 32) regardless of Mining level
rewards.items: Golden nuggets spent at Prospector Percy's Nugget Shop on the Prospector outfit (helmet/jacket/legs/boots, each a small flat xp-boost %), coal bag, gem bag, plus in-mine upgrades: upper floor access (100 nuggets, needs Mining 57), Super Hopper (50), pay-dirt sack capacity 108->189 (200)
rewards.unlocks: Prospector helmet required for a hard Falador Diary task

notes: Low-intensity, low-barrier (Mining 30, no quest) Mining trainer that doubles as the only source of the coal bag (a major Blast Furnace xp/gp multiplier) and gem bag -- so completing Motherlode Mine's nugget grind feeds directly into Blast Furnace efficiency later. Recommended purchase order is upper-floor+hopper unlocks first (best xp/attention), then outfit pieces by xp-bonus-per-nugget, sack capacity last. Full outfit + upper level + bag upgrades costs roughly 17,500 pay-dirt (Mining ~30 to ~68) to fund via nuggets alone.

name: Hallowed Sepulchre

skills.agility: 52 (unboostable) to enter floor 1, rising per floor: 62/72/82/92 for floors 2-5

quests: Sins of the Father (for Darkmeyer access)
items: recommended: Hitpoints 70+, a Saradomin-affiliated item to use the Magical Obelisks without draining run energy

rewards.xp: Agility xp from traversing obstacles per floor (rate rises with floor difficulty); coffin-looting is optional side content gated by its own skill checks (Thieving 66 boostable, Construction 56 boostable for bridges, Prayer 54 for braziers, Magic 7 for portal frames, Ranged 62 for pillars)
rewards.items: Hallowed marks from coffins spend in the Mysterious Hallowed Goods shop: Hallowed crystal shard (lobby teleport), Hallowed token (+1 min per attempt), Hallowed grapple/focus/symbol/hammer (guaranteed obstacle-pass tools for pillars/portals/braziers/bridges), Hallowed ring (fail-safe teleport-back, cuts food need), Dark dye (recolours Graceful to Hallowed Graceful, 6 needed for full set), Hallowed sack (bonus loot rolls)
rewards.unlocks: Giant squirrel Agility pet (rate improves 1/35,000 on floor 1 up to 1/2,000 on floor 5)

notes: The premier late-game Agility trainer/course once Sins of the Father is done -- unboostable per-floor Agility gates (52/62/72/82/92) mean it scales naturally alongside a player's Agility level rather than being a single unlock. Its reward-shop tools (grapple/focus/symbol/hammer) make later runs progressively faster/safer once bought, and it is the only source of the Hallowed Graceful recolour and (via coffins) supplementary Thieving/Construction/Prayer/Magic/Ranged practice tied to obstacle skill-checks.

name: Mahogany Homes

skills.construction: 1 for Beginner tier; Novice 20, Adept 50, Expert 70 to unlock higher (boostable) contract tiers

items: regular/oak/teak/mahogany planks depending on contract tier (nails not required)

rewards.xp: Construction xp per contract scales sharply by tier: Beginner 500 (512.5 with carpenter's outfit), Novice 1,250, Adept 2,250, Expert 2,750 (2,818.7 with outfit); experienced players complete ~50-60 contracts/hour with a plank sack + efficient teleports/NPC Contact
rewards.items: Carpenter points -> Mahogany Homes Reward Shop: supply crates (Construction materials), Amy's saw (equipable, priority buy), plank sack (holds 28 planks, top priority for continued training efficiency), Hosidius blueprints (unlocks Hosidius Wall Kit), full Carpenter's outfit (helmet/shirt/trousers/boots, further boosts contract xp)

notes: Repeatable Construction trainer with almost no entry barrier (Construction 1 for the lowest tier) that scales cleanly with level via 4 boostable tiers up to 70 -- unlike a player-owned house grind, materials are semi-consumed via contracts but return carpenter points and player-facing outfit bonuses that compound xp/hour. The plank sack and Amy's saw are the key early reward-shop purchases since they most directly raise contracts/hour; full completion (975 Expert contracts for the collection log) is a long-tail late-game grind.

name: Achievement Diary

rewards.effect: 492 tasks spread across 12 regions x 4 tiers (Easy/Medium/Hard/Elite). A tier's rewards can only be claimed from that diary's own taskmaster once every task in that tier AND every preceding tier of the same region is complete (tasks themselves can be done out of order). Every tier claim always includes an antique lamp + an equippable armour/cape piece, plus passive perks: xp-rate boosts, shop discounts, new teleport unlocks, and region access. Completing every diary at every tier unlocks purchase of the achievement diary cape (99,000gp, Twiggy O'Korn, Draynor Village), which itself lets the quest point cape be trimmed cyan and is one of two requirements for the music cape's black trim.

notes: Own words, system-level rather than per-region: this is the requester's own named steer-point pattern (CONSOLIDATED.md pattern 12, 'QP/diary as progression metric & anchor') so it is marked hard rather than soft. Concretely non-obvious hard-gate found in this pass: the aggregate 'All requirements' tables (Achievement_Diary.s2, one per tier via a shared DiarySkillStats+quest-list tabber) list 'Acquire a prospector helmet from the Motherlode Mine' as an Additional-requirement checkbox for BOTH the Hard tier AND the Elite tier bundles (i.e. across every region's Hard/Elite diary, not one specific region) -- only ONE prospector helmet piece is needed to satisfy this box, not the full 4-piece set, but it means the Prospector kit unlock (unlockdb:prospector-kit, this same batch) is a literal cross-dependency baked into the diary system, not just a coincidentally-useful item. Other Hard/Elite aggregate gates worth flagging for planner interleaving: Warriors' Guild access (130 combined Attack+Strength or 99 in either), Wilderness God Wars Dungeon access, at least one god spell, and (Elite only) full completion of Chambers of Xeric, TzHaar Fight Cave, and 'all quests' -- these functionally chain the diary system to the raid/quest-cape burndown too.

name: Angler outfit

items: Fishing Trawler minigame drop (1/12 chance per game of a NOT-yet-owned piece, contribution bar must be >=50 when the game ends; average ~48 games / ~4h48m for the full set), OR purchase from Alry the Angler for 100 Molch pearls per piece (400 total) via aerial fishing -- explicitly the slower, not-recommended path (20+ hours)

rewards.effect: Full set: +2.5% Fishing xp on all Fishing activities (does not apply to drift net fishing or deep sea trawling). More importantly the outfit is a hard functional requirement, not just an xp multiplier, for three separate things: a master clue step, the 'Dress Like You Mean It' hard combat achievement, and access to minnow fishing.

notes: Own words: upgradeable to the spirit angler's outfit by trading each piece + 1,200 spirit flakes (4,800 total) to Gita Prymes in the Ruins of Unkah, which lets the outfit double as a tether rope when fighting Tempoross. Time-investment section on the source page explicitly warns the outfit is NOT worth farming purely for the xp bonus against faster training methods (3-tick barbarian fishing at 65, 3-tick fly fishing/Tempoross-with-crystal-harpoon at 80) -- own-words break-even is only worthwhile below ~66,129 xp/hr. Marked hard rather than soft specifically because of the minnow-fishing/clue-step/combat-achievement functional gates, which cannot be bypassed by training rate the way the xp bonus can.

name: Ardougne Diary

quests: Rune Mysteries (task 1: right-click teleport at Wizard Cromperty)
Biohazard (task 6: enter the Combat Training Camp north of West Ardougne)

rewards.effect: Tier-1 Ardougne cloak: unlimited teleport to the Ardougne Monastery. Higher Ardougne diary tiers (Medium/Hard/Elite) upgrade the same cloak to cloak 2/3/4, each adding further teleport destinations and passive perks -- this row covers only the Easy-tier cloak 1 unlock.

notes: Own words: reward is claimed automatically on finishing the 10th (final) Easy Ardougne Diary task, no separate NPC turn-in. Not a hard gate for anything else in the KB, but a high-leverage soft accelerant already cross-referenced by two other contributed rows: bg:pps-03-withdraw-herb-run cites the cloak as the closest teleport to the Ardougne herb patch (2667,3375), and Spirit_tree.s1's own text calls Ardougne cloak -> Ardougne Monastery -> Khazard Battlefield spirit tree (immediately NW) the cheapest, lowest-requirement route into the spirit-tree network before 83 Farming makes the Farming Guild tree reachable. Also grants (bundled Easy-diary rewards, not cloak-specific): one antique lamp worth 2,500xp in any skill 30+, and double death runes + auto-noted Jubster/Frogeel drops when trading cats to West Ardougne civilians.

name: Fairy ring

quests: Fairytale II - Cure a Queen (partial, up to 'granted permission')
items: Dramen staff or Lunar staff to activate a ring (waived if the player has completed the Elite Lumbridge & Draynor Diary)

rewards.effect: Unlocks the fairy ring transportation network: 55 of 64 possible A/B/C/D letter-code combinations are active landing points spread across almost every region of the game, each reached by dialling a 3-letter code at any ring. This is the single densest point-to-point travel web in the game and is treated as the default transport layer in nearly every other route/guide already mined into this KB (herb-patch runs, Chambers of Xeric access, Barrows, Mort Myre, penguin/Miscellania errands).

notes: Own words: the unlock is a quest-chain burndown in itself (5 quests + a partial 6th), not a single flag, so it should be modelled as its own multi-quest requisite node rather than folded silently into whichever quest happens to finish it. Once live it becomes a hard dependency for a large share of downstream travel steps already contributed elsewhere in this KB (travel:ref-fairy-ring, bg:pps-03-withdraw-herb-run, travel:milestone-raids-cox all cite fairy-ring codes as a named routing option) -- it is the earliest 'hard' access anchor a fresh account can reach, and most later route synthesis assumes it is already unlocked. Downstream acceleration: turns multi-region herb runs, raid access, and quest-hub travel from multi-minute walks/teleport-chains into a single 2-click ring dial.

name: Graceful outfit

items: 260 marks of grace (hood 35, top 55, legs 60, gloves 30, boots 40, cape 40), bought piece by piece from Grace at the Rogues' Den

rewards.effect: Full set: -25kg weight and +30% run-energy restoration rate. No skill/quest hard-gate to purchase -- only the mark-of-grace currency, earned by running rooftop Agility courses (best rate at Canifis, level 40, 16-18/hr with no level-over-20 penalty; Ardougne, level 90, reaches 22/hr with the elite Ardougne diary).

notes: Own words: pure QoL multiplier, nothing else in the KB treats it as a hard prerequisite, but it compounds efficiency across virtually every later travel/training step that involves running (less need for stamina potions, more inventory space free of energy items). Distinct from a separate, cheaper action: RECOLOURING an already-owned full set costs only 90 marks total (or 15/piece) paid to Osten in Shayzien, not the 260-mark Grace purchase -- do not conflate the two costs. Sell-back refund on uncoloured pieces to Grace is 80% (208 marks).

name: Kharedst's memoirs

quests: Client of Kourend (grants the blank book itself, from Veos at Port Piscarilius)
The Depths of Despair (torn page: Lunch by the lancalliums, teleports near the Hosidius Kitchen)
The Queen of Thieves (torn page: The fisher's flute, teleports centre of Port Piscarilius)
Tale of the Righteous (torn page: History and hearsay, teleports Graveyard of Heroes SW of Shayzien)
The Forsaken Tower (torn page: Jewellery of jubilation, teleports NE of the Lovakengj bank)
The Ascent of Arceuus (torn page: A dark disposition, teleports centre of Arceuus)

rewards.effect: Each of the 5 short Kourend quests adds one torn page to the memoirs, each page unlocking a 'Reminisce' teleport to its own city plus 20 teleport charges (stacking with further pages); cannot teleport past level 20 Wilderness. After A Kingdom Divided the book auto-upgrades to the Book of the dead (reclaimed from Commander Fullore instead of Veos), which per an existing KB row (travel:milestone-raids-cox) also sets up a two-way transport to Shayziens' Wall from the Chambers of Xeric mountain via the Mountain Guide.

notes: Own words: marked inline rather than hard/soft because none of the 5 gating quests exist solely to earn a memoirs page -- all 5 (Depths of Despair, Queen of Thieves, Tale of the Righteous, Forsaken Tower, Ascent of Arceuus) are ALSO direct quest requirements already contributed elsewhere for the Kourend & Kebos Medium diary and for A Kingdom Divided (questdb: rows confirm this), so the teleport network accrues automatically as a side effect of doing those quests for other reasons -- no dedicated grind/step needs scheduling for it. The book itself is a quest-item reward of Client of Kourend, reclaimable free from Veos (or Cabin Boy Herbert post-partial A Kingdom Divided) if lost, and retains charges through a destroy/reclaim cycle.

name: Player-owned house

items: 1,000 coins paid to any estate agent (default path), OR free automatically on completing the Daddy's Home miniquest (10 planks + 5 bolts of cloth + 16+ nails + a hammer/saw, all obtainable in-quest)

rewards.effect: Grants a personal, expandable house (Rimmington by default, relocatable to 8 cities for a fee at higher Construction) that is the entire focus of the Construction skill and doubles as the best travel hub in the game once built out: a portal chamber/nexus can hold teleports from every spellbook at once (no spellbook-switching needed), a fully-upgraded jewellery box gives unlimited ring of dueling/games necklace/skills necklace/combat bracelet/amulet of glory/ring of wealth teleports, and the 75-Construction superior garden's spirit-tree-and-fairy-ring hotspot is the single fastest access point to BOTH transport networks at once from one tile.

notes: Own words: also unlocks the ability to use experience lamps on Construction and to earn Construction xp from repairing Wintertodt braziers / Tempoross totem poles+masts / Vale Totem poles -- i.e. owning a POH is a hard prerequisite for gaining Construction xp from three separate group minigames, not just a nice-to-have room. Treated as a hard anchor because so much later infrastructure (occult altar spellbook-swap, gilded altar prayer training at 3.5x with both incense burners lit, costume room bank-space relief, mounted glory in the quest hall) is gated behind first getting the house at all, and several already-contributed KB rows (Daddy's Home reward text, herb-run teleport-choice notes) already assume a POH exists. Downstream acceleration: the free-via-Daddy's-Home path means a fresh account never needs to pay the 1,000gp if that short miniquest is slotted early.

name: Prospector kit

items: Golden nuggets from the Motherlode Mine, spent at Prospector Percy's shop: helmet 40, jacket 60, legs 50, boots 30 (180 total for the full set; 30 Mining to enter/mine in the Motherlode Mine itself)

rewards.effect: Mining xp boost per piece: helmet +0.4%, jacket +0.8%, legs +0.6%, boots +0.2%, plus a +0.5% set bonus for wearing all 4, totalling +2.5% Mining xp on the full set. Prospector armour does not increase golden-nugget drop chance itself. Buying the pricier pieces first is marginally more xp-efficient overall.

notes: Own words, non-obvious cross-dependency found this pass: the Achievement Diary system's own aggregate 'All requirements' tables (Achievement_Diary.s2) list 'Acquire a prospector helmet from the Motherlode Mine' as an Additional-requirement checkbox for BOTH the Hard tier AND Elite tier bundles across every region's diary -- only the helmet piece is needed to satisfy this, not the full 4-piece set, but it makes prospector kit a literal hard prerequisite baked into unlockdb:achievement-diary-system rather than a standalone cosmetic-xp buy. Marked hard on that basis even though the xp-boost effect alone would only justify soft.

name: Quest point cape

quests: All quests (every released quest, miniquests excluded)
items: 99,000 coins, paid to the Wise Old Man in Draynor Village (also buys the matching quest point hood)

rewards.effect: Unlimited teleport to the Legends' Guild gates -- the second-closest teleport to a fairy ring after a POH. Independent of whether the cape is worn, players holding max quest points also get every Nightmare Zone dream discounted 10,000 coins. Performing the cape's emote is itself a required task for the Elite Lumbridge & Draynor Diary (in the Wise Old Man's house) and the Elite Falador Diary (atop Falador Castle).

notes: Own words: this is the requester's own named capstone/checkpoint pattern (CONSOLIDATED.md pattern 12, QP as a progression anchor) and it directly cross-links to unlockdb:achievement-diary-system via the two elite-diary emote tasks above -- i.e. the quest cape and the diary system gate each other at the elite tier. If a new quest releases after the cape is obtained, it auto-unequips and cannot be re-equipped/operated until the new quest is completed (banked automatically if inventory is full; LOST and must be re-bought for 99,000gp if both inventory and bank are full when this happens) -- a real risk worth flagging for any planner treating 'quest cape obtained' as a terminal, static state rather than one that can regress on future quest releases. Trimming the cape (cyan) additionally requires the achievement diary cape (all diaries at all tiers), chaining this unlock to unlockdb:achievement-diary-system a second way.

name: Rogue's outfit

items: Rogue's equipment crates from successfully opening wall safes at the end of the Rogues' Den minigame (untradeable pieces, so 50 Agility + 50 Thieving are effectively required just to wear them even though the minigame itself has no stated level gate on the page fetched)

rewards.effect: Doubles pickpocketing loot: each of the 5 pieces (mask/top/gloves/trousers/boots) adds a flat +15% chance to double an NPC's pickpocket loot (15/30/45/60% for 1-4 pieces), and wearing all 5 together jumps straight to a guaranteed 100% -- not a linear extrapolation of the per-piece rate. Pieces are interchangeable (any 5 of the 5 slots filled = 100%, doesn't matter which). Does not affect chests, stalls, Pyramid Plunder, Hallowed Sepulchre, Underwater Agility and Thieving, or scroll-box drop rates; coin pouches/scroll boxes get a different doubling mechanic (chance at one extra pouch/coin value instead of a flat double).

notes: Own words: narrow QoL multiplier scoped entirely to pickpocket-based Thieving training/money-making, doesn't gate anything else in the KB. Storable in a POH costume room armour case (cross-references unlockdb:player-owned-house). Since 1 June 2017 the crates let players pick which piece to receive (previously fixed order until full set owned), which matters for Ultimate Ironmen who can no longer be RNG-locked out of the full set.

name: Spirit tree

rewards.effect: Unlocks the spirit-tree transportation network: 8 permanent stops (Tree Gnome Village, Tree Gnome Stronghold, Battlefield of Khazard, Grand Exchange, Feldip Hills, Poison Waste (partial Path of Glouphrie), Prifddinas (Song of the Elves), Laguna Aurorae (Pandemonium+58 Sailing)) plus 5 Farming-grown patches and a POH superior-garden tree at 75 Construction.

notes: Own words: gate is a single, cheap early quest (Tree Gnome Village, confirmed on Unlockable_content.s18's own table) versus fairy rings' 5-quest chain -- this makes spirit tree the FIRST regional teleport-web a fresh account can unlock, not merely a lesser alternative. It is not a hard dependency for anything else in the KB (nothing greps as requiring spirit-tree access), so it reads as a soft/optional accelerant, but a high-value one: source page's own text ranks fastest-access methods pre-83-Farming as Ardougne cloak -> Khazard Battlefield tree (zero cost, low reqs) or GE (ring of wealth/teletab + medium Varrock Diary), and calls the Achievement Diary Cape + Elder Gnome child route faster than even the POH tree despite being farther on foot (no POH load screen). Downstream acceleration: bridges the travel gap between tutorial-exit and the fairy-ring unlock chain being finished.

name: Xeric's talisman

items: No purchase/quest gate on the amulet itself -- it is a monster drop: 1/250 from lizardmen/lizardman brutes/lizardman shamans (improved to 1/125 after the easy Kourend & Kebos Diary), or 1/300 stealing from stone chests in the Lizardman Temple beneath Molch

rewards.effect: 4 free teleports by default (Xeric's Lookout SE of Shayzien, Xeric's Glade NE of the Hosidius farm patch, Xeric's Inferno at the Lovakengj lovakite furnace, Xeric's Heart at Kourend Castle) plus a 5th, Xeric's Honour at the Chambers of Xeric summit on Mount Quidamortem, which permanently unlocks only after using an Ancient tablet (a 1/10 drop from the post-raid Ancient chest) on the talisman.

notes: Own words: no quest/skill gate to obtain the base item, only RNG (or the diary-boosted RNG); reaching a lizardman spawn to farm it does benefit from prior Kourend region quests for fast travel but that is convenience, not a hard requirement. Cross-references existing KB row travel:milestone-raids-cox, which already lists 'xerics-talisman-post-first-clear' as one of three named routes into Chambers of Xeric, and diarydb:kourend-and-kebos-hard, whose own Hard-tier task list includes 'teleport to Xeric's Heart via Xeric's talisman' as a literal diary requirement -- i.e. owning the talisman is itself gated INTO one diary tier while also accelerating repeat access to a raid. Anchor_hint is soft rather than hard because nothing else in the KB requires possessing the talisman to proceed (Chambers of Xeric itself has alternate access routes: Lovakengj Minecart, fairy ring BLS).
